legendaryAcrobatDescription: Halve damage caused by falling. If wearing two pieces, you no longer suffer falling damage.
legendaryAssassinArmorDescription: Provides +2 Physical and Energy DR against attacks from human enemies.
legendaryAssassinWeaponDescription: Attacks gain the Vicious damage effect against humans. If already Vicious, inflict +2 damage per Effect rolled instead of +1.
legendaryAutoStimDescription: Once per scene, if HP < 1/4, automatically use a Stimpak (requires carrying one). Does not take an action.
legendaryBerserkerDescription: If Torso Physical DR is below 6, the weapon inflicts +1 CD damage for every point of Physical DR below 6.
legendaryBlazingDescription: When using the Defend action and missed by a melee attack, the attacker suffers 4 CD Persistent Energy damage.
legendaryBloodiedDescription: Inflicts +1 CD damage if current HP < Max HP, increasing to +2 CD (<3/4 HP), +3 CD (<1/2 HP), and +4 CD (<1/4 HP).
legendaryBolsteringDescription: Gain +2 Physical and Energy DR when HP < 1/2. Increases to +3 DR when HP < 1/4.
legendaryCavalierArmorDescription: When taking Defend or Sprint actions, gain +2 Physical DR to all locations until next turn.
legendaryCavalierWeaponDescription: When taking the Defend or Sprint actions, gain +2 Physical DR until the start of your next turn.
legendaryChameleonDescription: When hidden and not moving, enemies add +1 to the difficulty of tests to find you.
legendaryChampionDescription: Once per scene, you may set 1 die to a 1 on any STR or END test before rolling.
legendaryChargedDescription: When using the Defend action and missed by a melee attack, the attacker suffers 4 CD Stun Energy damage.
legendaryCloakingDescription: Once per scene, when hit by a melee attack, you become invisible (as a Stealth Boy).
legendaryCripplingDescription: When attacking an arm or leg, causes an injury if it inflicts four or more damage in one hit after reductions.
legendaryCryogenicDescription: Enemy melee attacks increase complication range by 1; on complication, attacker suffers X CD Freeze damage (X = their damage dice).
legendaryCunningDescription: Once per scene, you may set 1 die to a 1 on any AGI or PER test before rolling.
legendaryDeadeyeDescription: When taking the Aim action, additional minor and major actions cost 1 AP less for the rest of the turn (does not stack with Jet).
legendaryDefiantDescription: Once per scene, when spending max ammo for Fire Rate, declare weapon Empty to inflict +2 CD damage; requires minor action to reload.
legendaryDuelistArmorDescription: Enemy melee attacks increase complication range by 1; on complication, attacker's weapon is knocked from their hand.
legendaryDuelistWeaponDescription: When using Defend and missed by a melee attack, roll 1 CD; on an Effect, the attacker is disarmed.
legendaryEnragingDescription: When inflicting damage, spend 1 Luck to enrage the enemy, forcing them to attack the nearest enemy for the rest of the scene.
legendaryExplosiveDescription: Each Effect rolled hits a secondary target within Close range for half damage. Does not stack with Arc or Burst.
legendaryExterminatorArmorDescription: Provides +2 Physical and Energy DR against insects and mirelurks.
legendaryExterminatorWeaponDescription: Attacks gain the Vicious damage effect against insects and mirelurks.
legendaryFreezingDescription: When making an attack, you may spend a Luck point to add the Freeze damage effect.
legendaryFrigidDescription: When using the Defend action and missed by a melee attack, the attacker immediately suffers 4 CD Freeze Energy damage.
legendaryFuriousDescription: Each consecutive hit against an enemy already struck by this weapon in this scene adds +1 CD to the damage (cumulative).
legendaryGhoulSlayerArmorDescription: Provides +2 Physical and Energy DR against ghouls.
legendaryGhoulSlayerWeaponDescription: Attacks gain the Vicious damage effect against ghouls.
legendaryHitmanDescription: When taking the Aim action, the weapon inflicts +1 CD damage. Stacks with the Accurate quality.
legendaryHunterArmorDescription: Provides +2 Physical and Energy DR against animals and creatures.
legendaryHunterWeaponDescription: Attacks gain the Vicious damage effect against animals and creatures.
legendaryIncendiaryArmorDescription: Melee attackers suffer 2 CD Persistent Energy damage when they hit you.
legendaryIncendiaryWeaponDescription: Attacks gain the Persistent damage effect.
legendaryInstigatingDescription: If the target is at maximum HP, the weapon inflicts +3 CD damage.
legendaryIrradiatedDescription: The weapon gains the Radioactive damage effect.
legendaryJunkieDescription: Inflicts +1 CD damage for each addiction you are currently suffering from.
legendaryKneecapperDescription: Attacks against a leg cause a critical hit if they inflict 3 or more damage after reductions.
legendaryLuckyArmorDescription: Once per day, when spending Luck to reroll, you may set a d20 to a 1 or all CD to Effects instead.
legendaryLuckyWeaponDescription: After an attack where Luck was spent to reroll, roll 1 CD; on an Effect, regain one spent Luck point.
legendaryMartyrDescription: If HP < 1/4, reduce the AP cost to take additional actions in combat by 1.
legendaryMutantSlayerArmorDescription: Provides +2 Physical and Energy DR against super mutants.
legendaryMutantSlayerWeaponDescription: Attacks gain the Vicious damage effect against super mutants.
legendaryNimbleDescription: When taking the Aim action, you may also take a Move minor action for free.
legendaryNocturnalDescription: Between sunset and sunrise, attacks gain Vicious. If already Vicious, inflict +2 damage per Effect rolled instead of +1.
legendaryPenetratingDescription: Attacks gain Piercing 2. If already Piercing, increase the rating by +2.
legendaryPlasmaInfusedDescription: +2 CD damage and deals both Energy and Physical damage (uses target's lower resistance).
legendaryPoisonerWeaponDescription: Attacks gain the Persistent (Poison) damage effect.
legendaryPoweredDescription: Add 1 AP to the group pool at the start of each of your turns in combat.
legendaryPowerfulMightyDescription: Attacks with this weapon inflict +2 CD damage.
legendaryPunishingDescription: When hit by a melee attack, each Effect rolled on the damage roll inflicts 1 damage (same type) to the attacker.
legendaryQuickdrawDescription: Drawing this weapon does not count as a minor action and does not cost AP.
legendaryRadPoweredDescription: Once per scene, if you have Rad damage, set 1d20 to 1 on a STR test. +1 use per 5 Rad damage.
legendaryRelentlessDescription: On a critical hit, spend 1 Luck point to immediately regain any AP spent on that attack.
legendarySafecrackerDescription: Once per scene, you may set 1d20 to a 1 on any Lockpick test before rolling.
legendarySentinelArmorDescription: Gain +3 Physical and Energy DR so long as you did not move in your previous turn.
legendarySentinelWeaponDescription: Gain +2 Physical and Energy DR if you did not move in your previous turn. Increases to +3 if weapon has Parry.
legendarySharpDescription: Once per scene, you may set 1d20 to a 1 on any INT or CHA test before rolling.
legendarySprinterDescription: Reduce the AP cost to cross difficult terrain or obstacles by 1 (minimum 0).
legendaryStaggeringDescription: This weapon gains the Stun damage effect.
legendaryStalkerDescription: On a sneak attack with Aim and no other actions, replace 1d20 with a die that counts as a pre-rolled 1.
legendarySteadfastDescription: Taking the Aim action increases Physical DR by +2 until the start of your next turn.
legendaryTroubleshooterArmorDescription: Provides +2 Physical and Energy DR against robots.
legendaryTroubleshooterWeaponDescription: Attacks gain the Vicious damage effect against robots.
legendaryTwoShotDescription: Gains Vicious, +2 CD damage, and Ammo-Hungry (2) quality.
legendaryUnyieldingDescription: While HP are below 1/4, you may re-roll 1d20 on any test you attempt.
legendaryViolentDescription: +2 CD damage and causes injury on 4+ damage; gains Recoil (7) or adds +1 to existing Recoil.
legendaryWoundingDescription: This weapon gains the Persistent (Physical) damage effect.
medsAddictolDescription: A powerful and effective pre-War medicine which cleanses the body of the effects of addiction and the withdrawal symptoms that accompany it. It doesn't, however, prevent subsequent addictions, especially in the cases of long-term or habitual drug abuse. It can take some time to work, especially in severe cases, and tends to have a few side-effects, such as nausea and dizziness, while it is taking effect.
medsAntibioticsDescription: A dose of potent, broad-spectrum antibiotics that'll help clear out pretty much any infection or disease. Taking too many can be damaging to health overall, but these are rare enough that taking too many is rarely a problem.
medsBerryMentatsDescription: A portion of Mentats reformulated to promote brain activity and memory. Mentat Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Mentats. You increase the difficulty of all CHA tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Mentat (ordinary Mentats, or the Berry, Grape, or Orange versions).
medsBuffjetDescription: A potent, if highly addictive mixture of Buffout and Jet, providing a boost to physical prowess, heightened reflexes, and a burst of adrenaline. Buffout Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Buffout. You increase the difficulty of all STR and END tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Buffout (Buffout, Buffjet, or Bufftats).
medsBuffoutDescription: A powerful and quick-acting steroid which gained popularity with athletes prior to the Great War. For a few minutes, it makes one stronger and tougher. Buffout Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Buffout. You increase the difficulty of all STR and END tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Buffout (Buffout, Buffjet, or Bufftats).
medsBufftatDescription: An addictive cocktail of Buffout steroids and Mentats, heightening both physical prowess and awareness. Buffout Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Buffout. You increase the difficulty of all STR and END tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Buffout (Buffout, Buffjet, or Bufftats).
medsCalmexDescription: A light tranquilizer used to calm the nerves. It isn't potent enough to function as a painkiller, but a dose can quiet anxieties and fears, keeping panic from disturbing delicate activities. Calmex Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Calmex. You suffer complications on AGI tests on all rolls of 18 or higher whenever you are not under the effects of Calmex.
medsDaddyODescription: Popular with beatniks and intellectuals before the Great War, Daddy-O heightens the user's cognitive faculties, making them more alert and more able to process information, but users tend to hyper-focus on the tasks in front of them, making it awkward to interact with them. Daddy-O Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Daddy-O. You increase the difficulty of all PER and INT tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of Daddy-O.
medsDaytripperDescription: A mild pre-War relaxant and hallucinogen, Day Tripper was favored by Americans seeking a brief escape from reality. The resulting high made users more laid-back and relaxed, while also making them less inclined to exert themselves physically. It was popular amongst many social groups during the stresses of the Great War, food riots, and general societal problems in the years before the bombs fell. Day Tripper Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Day Tripper. You increase the difficulty of all CHA and LCK tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of Day Tripper.
medsDilutedRadAwayDescription: An intravenous drug which purges radiation from the user's body, absorbing radiation as it circulates through the user's bloodstream. It's also a potent diuretic, expelling the radiation with the user's urine, so it's advisable to drink a lot of (clean) water shortly after taking a dose. Sometimes encountered in a diluted form, reducing its effectiveness as the user is only taking a partial dose. Diluted RadAway is only about half as effective, but it's easier to find. Use: RadAway can be used in one of two ways: it can be applied using the Take Chem minor action, healing 4 Radiation damage immediately, or it can be applied as part of a First Aid action, healing 4 Radiation damage in addition to any other healing done (remove Radiation damage before applying other healing effects). Diluted RadAway works in the same way, but only heals 2 Radiation damage.
medsDilutedRadXDescription: A preventative medicine for those intending to enter areas with heightened risk of radiation exposure. A dose can help you resist the effects of radiation, especially when combined with other protective measures. Due to scarcity, it is often found diluted, reducing the effectiveness of a dose but allowing limited supplies to last longer. Diluted Rad-X is about half as effective, but easier to find.
medsDilutedStimpakDescription: A stimulation delivery package, or Stimpak, is a form of fast-acting medication used to bolster the body's own healing and recuperative ability, and countless millions were produced for household and workplace first aid kits, hospitals, clinics, and other places. A wonder of pre-War science, a Stimpak is a single-use syringe filled with a variety of potent healing agents, stimulants, and painkillers, and can be applied in an intravenous or intramuscular fashion: literally stick it anywhere and it'll work. Even so, it a Stimpak is most effective when applied by an Auto-Doc or someone with medical training alongside other treatment. Sometimes encountered in a diluted form, reducing its effectiveness as the user is only taking a partial dose. Diluted Stimpaks are only about half as effective, but they're easier to find. Use: A Stimpak can be used in one of two ways: it can be applied using the Take Chem minor action, healing 4 HP or treating an Injury immediately, or it can be applied as part of a First Aid action, healing 4 HP or treating an Injury in addition to any other healing done. Diluted Stimpaks work in the same way, but only heal 2 HP, though they can still treat Injuries as normal.
medsFuryDescription: An extremely powerful combat stimulant, Fury grants users a sense of invincibility, rendering them nearly immune to pain and recklessly dangerous in melee combat, while also rendering them largely oblivious to everything else as well. Fury Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Fury. You increase the difficulty of all STR and PER tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of Fury.
medsGrapeMentatsDescription: A tin of Mentats, their formula altered to reduce social anxiety, boost confidence, and make users more aware of body language and other social cues. Mentat Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Mentats. You increase the difficulty of all CHA tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Mentat (ordinary Mentats, or the Berry, Grape, or Orange versions).
medsHealingSalveDescription: A salve or ointment which can be applied to reduce pain and speed recovery from injury, typically made from several natural herbal ingredients. Use: Healing salve can be used in one of two ways: it can be applied using the Take Chem minor action, healing 2 HP immediately, or it can be applied as part of a First Aid action, adding +2 to the number of HP healed overall.
medsJetDescription: Jet is an inhaled stimulant which creates an altered state of consciousness where time appears to slow, heightening reflexes and allowing the user to act more quickly during a moment of crisis. Jet Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Jet. You increase the difficulty of all AGI tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Jet (Jet, or Jet Fuel; Ultra Jet has a different addiction effect).
medsJetFuelDescription: A volatile variant of Jet, Jet Fuel provides a massive burst of energy in the user, allowing them to act more swiftly and decisively. Jet Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Jet. You increase the difficulty of all AGI tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Jet (Jet, or Jet Fuel; Ultra jet has a different addiction effect).
medsMedXDescription: Med-X is a potent opiate analgesic which significantly reduces both the perception of pain and the natural emotional response to pain. In short, it's a powerful painkiller. Med-X Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Med-X. You increase the difficulty of all AGI tests by +1, and suffer +1 DC additional damage from all physical attacks whenever you are not under the effects of Med-X.
medsMentatsDescription: Mentats were a popular recreational and performance-enhancing drug before the Great War, which enhance memory and speed mental processes. It was popular amongst students studying for exams, and amongst armchair philosophers and those in jobs requiring intensive thought. The effect lasts only a short time, leaving the user feeling tired and unfocused afterwards, and they are addictive by design, with withdrawal causing migraines and irritability. Mentat Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Mentats. You increase the difficulty of all CHA tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Mentat (ordinary Mentats, or the Berry, Grape, or Orange versions).
medsOrangeMentatsDescription: A variant of Mentats which heighten awareness and sensory acuity. Mentat Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Mentats. You increase the difficulty of all CHA tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Mentat (ordinary Mentats, or the Berry, Grape, or Orange versions).
medsOverdriveDescription: An enhanced form of Psycho which massively stimulates aggression, making the user more dangerous in combat. Overdrive Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Overdrive. You increase the difficulty of all STR and AGI tests by +1 whenever you are not under the effects of Overdrive.
medsPsychoDescription: A potent combat stimulant created by the U.S. Army to enhance the effectiveness of their soldiers. It's still common in the wasteland, as the post-War plant Hubflower has proven to replicate several of the ingredients of the pre-War version. Psycho stimulates aggression and dulls pain receptors, allowing the user to fight harder with less care for their own safety. Psycho Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Psycho. You increase the difficulty of all STR tests by +1 and suffer +1 DC damage from all physical attacks whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Psycho (ordinary Psycho, Psycho Jet, Psychobuff, or Psychotats).
medsPsychobuffDescription: A highly addictive cocktail of Psycho and Buffout steroids, combining the effects of the two drugs to produce a period of extreme strength, durability, and aggression. Psycho Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Psycho. You increase the difficulty of all STR tests by +1 and suffer +1 DC damage from all physical attacks whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Psycho (ordinary Psycho, Psycho Jet, Psychobuff, or Psychotats).
medsPsychojetDescription: A powerful, addictive cocktail of Psycho and Jet, which enhances aggression, dulls pain, and augment's a target's reflexes and energy levels. The effect lasts only for a short while—under a minute—but the effects are always an explosive burst of extreme violence. Psycho Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Psycho. You increase the difficulty of all STR tests by +1 and suffer +1 DC damage from all physical attacks whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Psycho (ordinary Psycho, Psycho Jet, Psychobuff, or Psychotats).
medsPsychotatDescription: A cocktail of Psycho and Mentats. The heightened awareness granted by the Mentats and the aggression and pain insensitivity somewhat cancel one another out, but the resultant effects are a short period of high aggression, enhanced sensory acuity, and boosted pain tolerance. Psycho Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Psycho. You increase the difficulty of all STR tests by +1 and suffer +1 DC damage from all physical attacks whenever you are not under the effects of a type of Psycho (ordinary Psycho, Psycho Jet, Psychobuff, or Psychotats).
medsRadAwayDescription: An intravenous drug which purges radiation from the user's body, absorbing radiation as it circulates through the user's bloodstream. It's also a potent diuretic, expelling the radiation with the user's urine, so it's advisable to drink a lot of (clean) water shortly after taking a dose. Sometimes encountered in a diluted form, reducing its effectiveness as the user is only taking a partial dose. Diluted RadAway is only about half as effective, but it's easier to find. Use: RadAway can be used in one of two ways: it can be applied using the Take Chem minor action, healing 4 Radiation damage immediately, or it can be applied as part of a First Aid action, healing 4 Radiation damage in addition to any other healing done (remove Radiation damage before applying other healing effects). Diluted RadAway works in the same way, but only heals 2 Radiation damage.
medsRadXDescription: A preventative medicine for those intending to enter areas with heightened risk of radiation exposure. A dose can help you resist the effects of radiation, especially when combined with other protective measures. Due to scarcity, it is often found diluted, reducing the effectiveness of a dose but allowing limited supplies to last longer. Diluted Rad-X is about half as effective, but easier to find.
medsSkeetoSpitDescription: Derived from the secretions of a Bloodbug, and the curative properties of some wasteland plants, Skeeto Spit is a wasteland remedy which dulls the user's ability to feel pain. It's effective, lasts a reasonable amount of time, and is non-addictive, but it normally has to be homemade as it isn't generally produced in large quantities.
medsStimpakDescription: A stimulation delivery package, or Stimpak, is a form of fast-acting medication used to bolster the body's own healing and recuperative ability, and countless millions were produced for household and workplace first aid kits, hospitals, clinics, and other places. A wonder of pre-War science, a Stimpak is a single-use syringe filled with a variety of potent healing agents, stimulants, and painkillers, and can be applied in an intravenous or intramuscular fashion: literally stick it anywhere and it'll work. Even so, it a Stimpak is most effective when applied by an Auto-Doc or someone with medical training alongside other treatment. Sometimes encountered in a diluted form, reducing its effectiveness as the user is only taking a partial dose. Diluted Stimpaks are only about half as effective, but they're easier to find. Use: A Stimpak can be used in one of two ways: it can be applied using the Take Chem minor action, healing 4 HP or treating an Injury immediately, or it can be applied as part of a First Aid action, healing 4 HP or treating an Injury in addition to any other healing done. Diluted Stimpaks work in the same way, but only heal 2 HP, though they can still treat Injuries as normal.
medsStimpakDiffuserDescription: An enhanced version of the standard Stimpak, designed to disperse healing medication to multiple targets within close range. A Stimpak Diffuser can be used to heal 4 HP to all allies within Close range using the Take Chem minor action.
medsSuperStimpakDescription: A super Stimpak is an enhanced version of the standard Stimpak, with an additional vial of medicine and a leather strap to secure the needle to the patient's arm during treatment. A more potent cocktail of medication is used in a super Stimpak, allowing it to heal much more effectively. Use: A super Stimpak can be used in one of two ways: it can be applied using the Take Chem minor action, healing 8 HP or treating up to two Injuries immediately, or it can be applied as part of a First Aid action, healing 8 HP or treating up to two Injuries in addition to any other healing done.
medsUltraJetDescription: An extremely concentrated form of Jet, it is significantly more potent, and affects Ghouls as easily as it does humans due to its potency. Ultra Jet Addiction: A failed addiction roll renders you addicted to Ultra Jet. You increase the difficulty of all AGI tests by +1, and you generate one fewer AP whenever you succeed at a skill test (minimum 0) whenever you are not under the effects of Ultra Jet. This addiction is permanent and cannot be cured by any known means.
medsXCellDescription: X-cell was a general-purpose performance enhancer still being developed before the Great War. Development was never finished, but prototype versions were distributed through the black market and by scavengers after the War. It enhances all aspects of a user's abilities for a few minutes, but it is extremely addictive.

miscBackpackLargeDescription: Available in multiple sizes, backpacks are invaluable items for carrying more gear. A proper backpack allows for easier and more efficient transport of items.
miscBackpackSmallDescription: Available in multiple sizes, backpacks are invaluable items for carrying more gear. A proper backpack allows for easier and more efficient transport of items.
miscBobbyPinDescription: Flexible two-pronged clips made of metal or plastic. Before the Great War, they were used to keep hair in place, but they are equally useful for picking locks.
miscCommonJunkDescription: Common materials include wood, steel, plastic, rubber, cloth, concrete, bone, and ceramic: substances that are easy to find and recover. A single unit of common materials costs 1 cap and weighs 1 lb.
miscDeluxeToolboxDescription: A heavy but technically portable metal box containing a wide range of tools for repairing various electronic and mechanical devices. With the right tool, most routine repairs become much simpler.
miscDoctorBagDescription: A bag well-stocked with medicines and tools for the science of healing. While bulky to carry, those you save will be grateful. It can also hold disposable medical supplies like Stimpaks and RadAway, which are tracked separately.
miscElectronicLockpickDescription: Hard to find in the Wasteland, the Wattz Electronics Micromanipulator is a powerful tool capable of opening mechanical and electronic locks better than a human. However, it is delicate and can break if misused.
miscFirstAidKitDescription: A package full of bandages, antiseptic spray, and other tools for cleaning and dressing wounds in the field. It often contains Stimpaks and RadAway, though these are tracked separately.
miscFlareDescription: A small pyrotechnic tool that burns with a brilliant flame, generating bright light for a short period. They are extremely hot and can be used to ignite flammable substances.
miscFlashlightDescription: A durable battery-powered energy source that is easily pointed where needed. With powerful, long-lasting batteries, the light can last a long time.
miscGeigerCounterDescription: Relatively simple instruments used to detect ionizing radiation. The level is shown on a small gauge and signaled by a characteristic clicking sound used to easily monitor radiation.
miscHolotagDescription: A small, durable identification tool used by the Brotherhood of Steel. They contain information to identify the wearer upon death and function as a 'black box' for Power Armor data.
miscHolotapePlayerDescription: A small device for playing audio recorded on holotapes. Sound is played through a small speaker or attached headphones. It cannot play video or data tapes as it lacks a screen.
miscLanternDescription: A simple oil or gas lamp containing a flame fed by a fuel reservoir. It illuminates the surrounding area and has seen a resurgence in use due to the lack of reliable electrical power.
miscLocksmithToolsDescription: A rolled leather case containing a variety of lockpicks and tension wrenches for opening mechanical locks. It is more durable than improvised counterparts but can become useless if too many components break.
miscMultitoolDescription: A very useful small tool containing pliers, wire cutters, hex keys, and screwdrivers all in one. Ideal for those who need basic tools but want to travel light.
miscPackBrahminDescription: A large, two-headed mutant cow used as a beast of burden. It can carry up to 250 kg. It can be milked once a day and also produces units of fertilizer (unusual material) daily.
miscPipBoyDescription: A Personal Information Processor made by RobCo and Vault-Tec. Features include a status indicator, logbook, compass, radio, Geiger counter, and holotape player. Provides V.A.T.S. assistance in combat.
miscRadioDescription: A device capable of picking up radio signals and translating them into intelligible sound. Useful for recreation or emergency messages, provided it receives a signal.
miscRareJunkDescription: Rare materials are the most elusive and difficult to recover. Asbestos, ballistic fiber, circuitry, fiber optics, corrosive and antiseptic chemicals, and even nuclear material can be found in many items but are difficult to salvage. A single unit of rare materials costs 5 caps and weighs 1 lb.
miscTorchDescription: A long branch or piece of scrap metal with one end wrapped in cloth soaked in flammable liquid. A valid improvised light source that can be reused a few times.
miscUncommonJunkDescription: Uncommon materials are rarer and harder to recover, including copper, aluminum, leather, and silver, as well as cork, glass, fertilizer, fiberglass, and small components like gears, springs, and screws. A single unit of uncommon materials costs 3 caps and weighs 1 lb.
modAsbestosLiningDescription: +3 Energy Resistance, Ignores Persistent Energy damage
modAutomaticBarrelDescription: -1 damage DC, loses Close Quarters, increases Range by 1 grade, +1 Fire Rate
modAutomaticDescription: -1 damage DC, +2 Fire Rate, gains Burst, gains Inaccurate
modAutomaticPistonReceiverDescription: +2 Fire Rate, reduces Range by 1 grade
modAutomaticSignalRepeaterDescription: +2 Fire Rate, gains Burst, loses Blast
modBalancedDescription: Melee and unarmed attacks gain Piercing 1
modBayonetDescription: Melee Weapon, deals 4 DC Physical Piercing 1 damage
modBayonetMissileLauncherDescription: Melee Weapon, deals 4 DC Physical Piercing 1 damage
modBeamSplitterDescription: -1 damage DC, gains Spread, -1 Fire Rate, gains Inaccurate, reduces Range by 1 grade
modCompressionNozzleDescription: +1 damage DC, increases Range by 1 grade
modDeepDishDescription: +1 damage DC, increases Range by 1 grade
modElectricSignalAntennaDescription: Damage becomes 7 DC Energy, gains Radioactive
modElectrificationModuleDescription: Damage type becomes Energy, gains Radioactive
modElectrifiedBatonDescription: +2 damage DC, damage type becomes Energy
modElectrifiedSerratedSwordDescription: +1 damage DC, damage type becomes Energy, gains Persistent (Physical)
modElectrifiedSwordDescription: +1 damage DC, damage type becomes Energy
modFinnedBarrelDescription: +1 damage DC, increases Range by 1 grade
modFiveChargeCapacitorDescription: +3 damage DC, consumes 5 ammo per shot
modFlamerBarrelDescription: -2 damage DC, +2 Fire Rate, gains Burst, reduces Range by 1 grade, gains Inaccurate
modFourChargeCapacitorDescription: +2 damage DC, consumes 4 ammo per shot
modFullStockEnergyDescription: Gains Two-Handed, gains Piercing 1, loses Close Quarters
modHeatingPowerFistDescription: +2 damage DC, damage type becomes Energy
modHeatingSuperSledgeDescription: +1 damage DC, damage type becomes Energy
modLongBarrelEnergyDescription: Loses Close Quarters, increases Range by 1 grade
modLongNightVisionScopeDescription: Gains Precision, gains Night Vision, increases Range by 1
modLongNightVisionScopeEnergyDescription: Gains Precision, gains Night Vision, increases Range by 1 grade
modLongReinforcedBarrelDescription: Loses Close Quarters, increases Range by 1 grade, allows muzzle mod
modLongScopeEnergyDescription: Gains Precision, increases Range by 1 grade
modMarksmanStockDescription: Gains Two-Handed, loses Inaccurate, gains Precision
modMarksmanStockEnergyDescription: Gains Two-Handed, loses Inaccurate, gains Precision, loses Close Quarters
modMuffledDescription: Can reroll one d20 on Sneak checks
modPneumaticDescription: Stun damage requires 2+ Effects to affect character
modPortedBarrelDescription: Increases Range by 1 grade, +1 Fire Rate
modReceiver45Description: Damage becomes 4 DC, +1 Fire Rate, Ammo becomes .45
modReceiver50Description: Damage becomes 8 DC, gains Vicious, Ammo becomes .50
modRecoilCompensatingStockDescription: Gains Two-Handed, loses Inaccurate, +1 Fire Rate
modRecoilCompensatingStockEnergyDescript: Gains Two-Handed, loses Inaccurate, +1 Fire Rate, loses Close Quarters
modReflexSightDescription: Can reroll the Hit Location die
modReflexSightEnergyDescription: Can reroll the Hit Location die
modReflexSightGatlingDescription: Can reroll the Hit Location die
modRobotActivationFrameArmsDescription: Armor equipped with actuators and motor systems for arms. Melee attacks deal +1 DC damage.
modRobotActivationFrameBodyDescription: Armor equipped with actuators and motor systems for the body.
modRobotActivationFrameOpticsDescription: Armor equipped with actuators and motor systems for optics.
modRobotActivationFrameThrustersDescript: Armor equipped with actuators and motor systems for thrusters. Allows both a minor Movement action and a major Sprint action in the same turn.
modRobotBasePlatingArmsDescription: Supplemental handcrafted armor for arms that provides protection from physical hazards.
modRobotBasePlatingBodyDescription: Supplemental handcrafted armor for the body that provides protection from physical hazards.
modRobotBasePlatingOpticsDescription: Supplemental handcrafted armor for optics that provides protection from physical hazards.
modRobotBasePlatingThrustersDescription: Supplemental handcrafted armor for thrusters that provides protection from physical hazards.
modRobotFactoryArmorArmsDescription: Mass-produced armor that provides additional storage compartments for arms.
modRobotFactoryArmorBodyDescription: Mass-produced armor that provides additional storage compartments for the main body.
modRobotFactoryArmorOpticsDescription: Mass-produced armor that provides additional storage compartments for optics.
modRobotFactoryArmorThrustersDescription: Mass-produced armor that provides additional storage compartments for thrusters.
modRobotFactoryStorageBodyDescription: Factory armor with additional storage compartments to increase carrying capacity.
modRobotHydraulicFrameArmsDescription: Armor concealing a powerful hydraulic system for arms. Melee attacks deal +1 DC damage and gain the Stun effect.
modRobotHydraulicFrameBodyDescription: Armor concealing a powerful hydraulic system for the body.
modRobotHydraulicFrameOpticsDescription: Armor concealing a powerful hydraulic system for optics.
modRobotHydraulicFrameThrustersDescripti: Armor concealing a powerful hydraulic system for thrusters.
modRobotKnurledPlatingArmsDescription: Coating with jagged and serrated edges for arms. Sharp points can injure attackers.
modRobotKnurledPlatingBodyDescription: Coating with jagged and serrated edges for the body. Sharp points can injure attackers.
modRobotKnurledPlatingOpticsDescription: Coating with jagged and serrated edges for optics. Sharp points can injure attackers.
modRobotKnurledPlatingThrustersDescripti: Coating with jagged and serrated edges for thrusters. Sharp points can injure attackers.
modRobotPlatingMisterGutsyDescription: Mister Gutsy base plating with better thermal absorption capabilities, more resistant against Energy weapons.
modRobotPlatingStandardDescription: Standard factory plating that all new Mister Handy models come with.
modRobotPoisonousPlatingArmsDescription: Handcrafted armor coated with dangerous toxic materials for arms.
modRobotPoisonousPlatingBodyDescription: Handcrafted armor coated with dangerous toxic materials for the body.
modRobotPoisonousPlatingOpticsDescriptio: Handcrafted armor coated with dangerous toxic materials for optics.
modRobotPoisonousPlatingThrustersDescrip: Handcrafted armor coated with dangerous toxic materials for thrusters.
modRobotToxicPlatingArmsDescription: Armor with dangerous radioactive metal coating for arms.
modRobotToxicPlatingBodyDescription: Armor with dangerous radioactive metal coating for the body.
modRobotToxicPlatingOpticsDescription: Armor with dangerous radioactive metal coating for optics.
modRobotToxicPlatingThrustersDescription: Armor with dangerous radioactive metal coating for thrusters.
modRobotVoltaicFrameArmsDescription: Armor equipped with additional conductors and capacitors for arms.
modRobotVoltaicFrameBodyDescription: Armor equipped with additional conductors and capacitors for the body.
modRobotVoltaicFrameOpticsDescription: Armor equipped with additional conductors and capacitors for optics.
modRobotVoltaicFrameThrustersDescription: Armor equipped with additional conductors and capacitors for thrusters.
modShredderDescription: Melee Weapon, deals 6 DC Physical Piercing 2 damage
modSixChargeCapacitorDescription: +4 damage DC, consumes 6 ammo per shot
modSniperBarrelDescription: +2 damage DC, loses Close Quarters, increases Range by 1 grade, -1 Fire Rate
modSplitterDescription: -1 damage DC, gains Spread, gains Inaccurate
modStabilizedDescription: When aiming and making a Ranged Attack, damage increases by +1 DC
modStandardStockDescription: Gains Two-Handed, loses Inaccurate, loses Close Quarters
modStreamlinedDescription: Can spend up to 4 AP for bonus melee damage
modStunningBatonDescription: +3 damage DC, damage type becomes Energy, gains Stun
modStunningSuperSledgeDescription: +2 damage DC, damage type becomes Energy, gains Stun
modStunningSwordDescription: +2 damage DC, damage type becomes Energy, gains Stun
modThreeChargeCapacitorDescription: +1 damage DC, consumes 3 ammo per shot
modVaporizerNozzleDescription: +2 damage DC, increases Range by 1 grade, -1 Fire Rate
modVentedBarrelDescription: Increases Range by 1 grade, +1 Fire Rate, gains Reliable
name: Name
notEnoughAmmoAlert: You don't have enough AMMO to do it!
notEnoughLuckAlert: You don't have enough LUCK to do it!
perkAccuracyDescription: Once per combat, you can reroll all Combat Dice on a single damage roll without spending Luck points.
perkActionBoyGirlDescription: When spending AP to perform an additional major action, the character does not suffer the increased skill check difficulty during the second action.
perkAdamantiumSkeletonDescription: When the character suffers damage, the amount of damage required for it to become a critical hit increases based on the rank in this perk. For example, possessing one rank in this perk, you suffer a critical hit from 6 or more damage, rather than 5 or more. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 3.
perkAdrenalineRushDescription: When the character's health is below the maximum value, consider the STR score as 10 for all skill checks based on STR or melee attacks.
perkAllNightLongDescription: During nighttime hours—after sunset and before sunrise—your hunger and thirst states do not deteriorate. Every two days you are starving, you gain 1 Fatigue instead of every day.
perkAmmosmithDescription: You can craft ammunition at a weapons workbench. At rank 1, you can craft ammo up to rarity 1. At rank 2, you can also dismantle ammo, regaining half the required materials. At rank 3, roll DC equal to 6 minus the ammo's rarity when crafting: each hit adds +1 ammo, each Effect doubles the quantity. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkAnimalFriendDescription: At rank 1, whenever a Mammal, Lizard, or Insect NPC creature attacks the PC, roll 1 Combat Die: if a result other than an Effect is obtained, the creature chooses not to attack, though it can still attack another character. At rank 2, you can attempt a CHA + Survival check with a difficulty of 2 as a major action. If the check succeeds, the animal treats the character as a friend and will attack anyone who threatens them. Powerful and Legendary animals are not affected by this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkAquaboyGirlDescription: Water is the character's ally. At rank 1, you no longer take Radiation damage from swimming in irradiated water and can hold your breath for twice the normal time. At rank 2, enemies add +2 to the difficulty of checks to detect the character while submerged underwater. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 3.
perkArcherDescription: When wielding a bow or crossbow, increase the weapon's damage by +1 DC. At rank 2, this increases to +2 DC. At rank 3, to +3 DC. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 7.
perkArmorerDescription: You can modify armor with armor mods. Each rank in this perk unlocks an additional level of modifications: rank 1 unlocks level 1 mods, rank 2 unlocks level 2, and so on. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkAttackDogDescription: Your companion dog is trained for combat. Attacks deal +1 damage.
perkAwarenessDescription: When performing the Aim minor action on a target at Close range, the PC identifies their weak points and can attack more efficiently. The next attack made against that target gains the Piercing 1 effect or improves the rank of any Piercing X damage effect possessed by 1.
perkBarbarianDescription: Physical Damage Resistance increases on all hit locations based on the character's STR. You do not receive the benefits of this perk while wearing Power Armor. STR 7–8: +1 Physical DR, STR 9–10: +2 Physical DR, STR 11+: +3 Physical DR.
perkBasherDescription: When making a melee attack with a pistol, the attack gains the Vicious damage effect.
perkBetterCriticalsDescription: When one or more damage is dealt to an enemy, you can spend 1 Luck point to automatically deal a critical hit, causing an injury.
perkBigLeaguerDescription: When making a melee attack with a two-handed weapon, the weapon gains the Vicious damage effect.
perkBlackWidowLadyKillerDescription: The Black Widow perk affects men and male characters, while Lady Killer affects women and female characters; otherwise, they are identical. When attempting a CHA-based skill check to influence a character of the chosen sex, you can reroll 1d20. Additionally, attacks deal +1 additional Combat Die of damage against characters of the chosen sex.
perkBlacksmithDescription: You can modify melee weapons with melee weapon mods. Each rank in this perk unlocks an additional level of modifications: rank 1 unlocks level 1 mods, rank 2 unlocks level 2, and so on. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkBlitzDescription: When the character enters the reach of an opponent and makes a melee attack against them in the same turn, they can reroll a d20 for the attack. At rank 2, they also deal +1 Combat Die damage with the attack. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 3.
perkBlockerDescription: When struck by a melee attack, count your Physical or Energy damage resistance (matching the attack type) as +1 higher. At rank 2, this increases to +2. At rank 3, to +3. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkBloodsuckerDescription: Consuming a Blood Pack (including Glowing Blood Packs and Irradiated Blood) now heals twice as many HP and moves your Thirst state towards quenched by one step. When consuming Irradiated Blood, you now only roll 1 DC to determine Radiation damage.
perkBloodyMessDescription: When the character deals a critical hit, roll 1 Combat Die: if the result is an Effect, deal an additional injury in a random location.
perkBodyguardsDescription: Each player character and companion (other than yourself) within Close range increases your Damage Resistance and Energy Resistance by +1.
perkBornSurvivorDescription: When you suffer damage that would reduce your HP to below one-quarter of your maximum, you may automatically use a Stimpak (once per scene). At rank 2, you may do this 2 times per scene. At rank 3, 3 times per scene. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 8.
perkBowBeforeMeDescription: When wielding a bow or crossbow, the weapon gains Piercing 1 (or adds +1 to any existing Piercing X damage effect).
perkBulletShieldDescription: When wielding a big gun, increase your damage resistance by +2. At rank 2, this increases to +3. At rank 3, to +4. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 6.
perkButchersBountyDescription: When you successfully butcher a dead animal, roll +1 DC and find additional portions of meat equal to the total rolled. At rank 2, roll +2 DC. At rank 3, +3 DC. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkCanDoDescription: When looting a place where food is found, the character gets 1 additional random food item without spending AP.
perkCannibalDescription: You may attempt to butcher a dead human (END + Survival, difficulty 0) to obtain one portion of human flesh, which restores 4 HP and is Irradiated. At rank 2, you may also butcher ghouls. At rank 3, also super mutants. Cannibalism can be addictive. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 9.
perkCapCollectorDescription: When buying or selling items, the character can increase or decrease the price of exchanged goods by 10%.
perkCenterOfMassDescription: When making a ranged attack, you can choose to hit the target's Torso (or equivalent for creatures using a different hit location table) without increasing the attack difficulty. Additionally, you can reroll 1d20 when making the attack check.
perkChemResistantDescription: At rank 1, roll one fewer Combat Die to determine if you develop a drug addiction, to a minimum of 0. At rank 2, you cannot develop a drug addiction. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkChemSqueezerDescription: When looting a place where medicines or substances are found, the character gets 1 additional randomly determined item without spending AP.
perkChemistDescription: Substances created by the PC last twice as long. Additionally, it unlocks formulas for substances that have this perk as a requirement.
perkClassFreakDescription: Whenever a mutated human NPC would attack you, roll 1 DC: on any result other than an Effect, the creature chooses not to attack you. Additionally, you may re-roll 1d20 on any CHA-based skill test to influence a mutated human.
perkColaNutDescription: When you drink a Nuka-Cola beverage, you regain twice as many HP as normal.
perkCommandoDescription: When making a ranged attack with any weapon with a Fire Rate of 3 or higher (except heavy weapons), add +1 Combat Die per rank to the weapon's damage. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 3.
perkCommunityOrganizerDescription: For each rank of this perk, your community gains +1 Food and +1 Defense per day, as long as the resource is maintained. Additionally add +1 DC to the Hunting, Gathering, and Scavenging settlement actions. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 3.
perkComprehensionDescription: After using the bonus provided by reading a magazine, roll 1 Combat Die. If the result is an Effect, you can use that bonus one more time.
perkConcentratedFireDescription: When making a ranged attack and spending ammo to increase damage, the character can reroll up to 3 Combat Dice for the damage roll.
perkContractorDescription: At rank 1, you can halve the materials required to construct something (minimum 1 unit per type, -2 Happiness), or double construction time to avoid the penalty. At rank 2, untrained settlers can take construction actions (-1 Happiness unless a skilled worker assists). At rank 3, settlers without the required perk can assist if at least one perk-holder participates. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkCovertOperatorDescription: When you make a ranged sneak attack with a small gun or energy weapon, the damage is increased by +2 DC. You cannot gain this benefit while wearing Power Armor.
perkCrackShotDescription: When you make a ranged attack with a one-handed ranged weapon after aiming, the weapon's range increases by one step. At rank 2, the weapon also gains the Accurate quality if it did not already have it. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 7.
perkDaringDescription: Whenever you make a skill check and purchase one or more d20s by giving Action Points to the gamemaster, the character can reroll 1d20 on that check. You cannot purchase this perk if you already possess Prudent.
perkDeadManSprintingDescription: When you take the Sprint action and your current HP is below half of your maximum HP, you may spend 1 AP to move one additional zone.
perkDemolitionExpertDescription: When making an attack using a weapon with the Blast quality, the attack gains the Vicious damage effect. Additionally, it unlocks formulas for Explosives that have this perk as a requirement.
perkDogmeatDescription: You are not alone in the wilderness. You have a companion dog as a friend and ally in times of danger. The dog's profile uses the same as Dogmeat [...] and is treated as an allied NPC creature under the PC's control.
perkDromedaryDescription: When you drink a beverage, it moves your Thirst state back up towards quenched by one more step than normal.
perkDryNurseDescription: When you succeed at a First Aid action in combat to stabilize a dying ally and you used a Stimpak as part of that action, roll 1 DC. If you roll an Effect, the Stimpak is not used up and can be used again.
perkEnforcerDescription: When you make a ranged attack with a shotgun and target a specific location, your attack gains the Debilitating quality.
perkEntomologistDescription: When making an attack against an NPC with the Insect keyword, the character's attack gains the Piercing 1 damage effect or improves the degree of any Piercing X effect of the weapon by +1.
perkEscapeArtistDescription: During combat, if no enemy can see you at the start of your turn, you may attempt an AGI + Sneak test (difficulty = number of enemies within Medium range) to hide. Once hidden, enemies no longer know your location. Enemies also do not gain bonuses to detect you if you take the Sprint action.
perkEvasiveDescription: At rank 1, when performing the Defend major action, the character reduces the skill check difficulty by 1. At rank 2, the AP cost to further increase Defense is reduced to 1 AP. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 6.
perkFastMetabolismDescription: When recovering one or more HP from a source other than rest, increase the total HP recovered by +1 per rank in this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 3.
perkFieldSurgeonDescription: When you use a Stimpak as part of a First Aid action, you may heal 3 HP per AP spent. When you use RadAway as part of a First Aid action, you may remove one additional Radiation damage per AP spent.
perkFireInTheHoleDescription: When you make a ranged attack with a thrown weapon with the Blast quality, the base difficulty of the attack is 1 rather than 2.
perkFireproofDescription: Gain +1 Energy damage resistance against fire-based weapons and weapons with the Blast quality. At rank 2, this increases to +2. At rank 3, to +3. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 7.
perkFortuneFinderDescription: Whenever you roll to determine the amount of money found, you get a larger amount. At rank 1, find +3 Combat Dice additional caps. At rank 2, find +6 Combat Dice additional caps. At rank 3, find +10 Combat Dice additional caps. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkGhostDescription: Whenever making an AGI + Stealth check in shadows or darkness, the first purchased additional d20 is free. The maximum limit of 5d20 still applies.
perkGhoulishDescription: Like a ghoul, your body heals when exposed to radiation. You regain 1 HP for every 4 Radiation damage suffered. At rank 2, 1 HP per 3 Radiation damage. At rank 3, 1 HP per 2 Radiation damage. Your maximum HP is still reduced by Radiation damage. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 8.
perkGladiatorDescription: When wielding a one-handed melee weapon, increase the weapon's damage by +1 DC. At rank 2, this increases to +2 DC. At rank 3, to +3 DC. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkGlowSightDescription: When you make an attack against a Glowing enemy, you inflict +1 DC damage. At rank 2, this increases to +2 DC. At rank 3, to +3 DC. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 8.
perkGoatLegsDescription: You have +2 Physical damage resistance against falling damage. At rank 2, this increases to +4. At rank 3, to +6. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkGreenThumbDescription: When you spend time foraging and roll on the Foraging table, you gather 2 items, plus an additional 2 for every AP spent.
perkGrimReaperDescription: When you kill one or more enemies with an attack, roll 1 Combat Die. If the result is an Effect, add 2 AP to the group pool.
perkGunFuDescription: When making a successful ranged attack, you can spend 1 AP and 1 ammo to hit a second target at Close range from the initial target. The second target suffers the same amount of damage as the initial target. At rank 2, you can spend 2 AP and 2 ammo to hit two additional targets. At rank 3, you can spend 3 AP and 3 ammo to hit three additional targets. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkGunNutDescription: You can modify small guns and heavy weapons with weapon mods. Each rank in this perk unlocks an additional level of modifications: rank 1 unlocks level 1 mods, rank 2 unlocks level 2, and so on. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkGunRunnerDescription: When you take the Sprint action while holding a one-handed ranged weapon, you may spend 1 AP to move one additional zone.
perkGunslingerDescription: When making an attack with a one-handed ranged weapon with a Fire Rate of 2 or less, increase the weapon's damage by +1 Combat Die for each rank. Additionally, you can reroll the die to determine the hit location. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkHackerDescription: The difficulty of skill checks to hack computers is reduced by 1, to a minimum of 0.
perkHappyCamperDescription: At rank 1, if your hunger state is full or sated and you have set up a camp with a heat source, your hunger does not deteriorate. At rank 2, if your thirst state is quenched or hydrated and you have set up a camp with a heat source, your thirst does not deteriorate.
perkHappyGoLuckyDescription: The first time in a scene when you consume an alcoholic beverage, regain 1 Luck point, up to your normal maximum.
perkHealerDescription: When healing a patient's HP using the First Aid action, increase the amount of HP healed by +1 for each rank of this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkHealingHandsDescription: When you succeed at a First Aid action in combat to stabilize a dying ally, that ally also removes Radiation damage equal to your Medicine skill rank.
perkHeaveHoDescription: When making an attack with a thrown weapon, you can spend 1 AP to increase the weapon's range by one level: from Close to Medium, or from Medium to Long.
perkHiredHelpDescription: You gain the ability to recruit a humanoid companion. Your companion can care for their own needs and recovers from injuries as a player character would.
perkHomeDefenseDescription: At rank 1, you can craft traps (complexity 2). If you fail the INT + Repair test, the trap misfires against you. At rank 2, a failed trap test does not misfire—the GM creates a complication instead. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkHomebodyDescription: You do not need to sleep in a settlement you belong to in order to recuperate. At rank 1, spending an hour there lets you roll DC equal to your Endurance and regain that many HP. At rank 2, you regain +1 HP per Effect rolled, and injury recovery difficulty is reduced to a maximum of 2. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkHunterDescription: When performed against an NPC with one of the Mammal, Lizard, Insect, or Mutated keywords, the PC's attack receives the Vicious damage effect, if it didn't already have it.
perkImprovedHealingDescription: When making a CON + Survival check to heal from injuries, the first additional d20 is free. The maximum limit of 5d20 still applies.
perkIncisorDescription: When wielding a melee weapon, the weapon gains Piercing 1 (or adds +1 to any existing Piercing X). At rank 2, this increases to Piercing 2 (or +2). Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 9.
perkInfiltratorDescription: When making a skill check to pick a lock on a door or container, you can reroll 1d20.
perkInspirationalDescription: As an exemplary role model, the maximum number of AP the group can store is increased by 1.
perkIntenseTrainingDescription: Increases any S.P.E.C.I.A.L. attribute by 1. As a rule, S.P.E.C.I.A.L. attributes cannot be increased beyond 10 using this method. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 2.
perkIronFistDescription: At rank 1, unarmed attacks deal +1 Combat Die damage. At rank 2, unarmed attacks also gain the Vicious damage effect. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkIroncladDescription: While wearing armor (except Power Armor), add +1 to your Physical and Energy damage resistance. At rank 2, this increases to +2. At rank 3, to +3. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkJunkShieldDescription: For every 5 items of Junk you carry, add +1 to your Physical and Energy damage resistance. The maximum bonus equals your rank in this perk. You do not gain this bonus while wearing Power Armor. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 8.
perkJunktownVendorDescription: The difficulty of any CHA + Barter check made to buy or sell goods is reduced by 1, to a minimum of 0.
perkLaserCommanderDescription: When the PC makes an attack with a ranged energy weapon, damage is increased by +1 Combat Die for each rank in this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkLeadBellyDescription: At rank 1, you can reroll Combat Dice to determine if Radiation damage is suffered from irradiated food or drinks. At rank 2, it grants immunity to Radiation damage from consuming irradiated food or drinks. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkLicensedPlumberDescription: Pipe weapons you use do not have the Unreliable quality.
perkLifeGiverDescription: Increases maximum health points by an amount equal to the character's Constitution value. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkLightStepDescription: When rolling dice to generate complications on an Agility-based skill check, you can ignore one complication for each AP spent. Additionally, you can reroll 1d20 on any AGI + Athletics check to avoid pressure-activated traps or similar mechanisms.
perkLocalLeaderDescription: At rank 1, you can establish supply lines between settlements where you have at least Friendly reputation. At rank 2, you can request stores and crafting tables in a settlement, unlocking Trading Stores, Armor Stores, Weapon Stores, and various workbenches. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkLockAndLoadDescription: When you make attacks with a big gun with a fire rate greater than 0, its fire rate increases by 1 per rank of this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkLoyalCompanionDescription: Your companion will never abandon you. Immune to fear effects.
perkMakeshiftDescription: Allows you to repair an object without needing to use any components. However, the repair is temporary and the object will break again upon the next complication suffered while being used. The complication range for all skill checks to use the object increases by 1, with a result of 19-20.
perkMartialArtistDescription: When you make a melee attack, you may take an additional major action by spending 1 AP rather than 2, so long as this additional action is also a melee attack with a currently equipped weapon.
perkMasterThiefDescription: When attempting to pick a lock or pickpocket someone, the difficulty of the opponent's skill check to detect the character is increased by +1.
perkMechanicalMenaceDescription: Whenever a robot NPC would attack you, roll 1 DC: on any result other than an Effect, the robot chooses not to attack you. Additionally, you may re-roll 1d20 on any CHA-based skill test to influence a robot.
perkMedicDescription: When using the First Aid action to try to treat an injury, the character can reroll 1d20.
perkMeltdownDescription: When you kill an enemy with an energy weapon, they explode. Roll a number of Combat Dice equal to half the weapon's damage (rounded down). For each Effect obtained, a creature within Close range of the exploded enemy (starting from the nearest) suffers Energy damage equal to the total Combat Dice obtained.
perkMisterSandmanDescription: When a sneak attack is made with a silenced or suppressed weapon, damage is increased by +2 Combat Dice. You do not receive the benefits of this perk while wearing Power Armor.
perkModernRenegadeDescription: When you make an attack with a one-handed ranged weapon without aiming, a successful attack scores one additional AP (which may not be saved). At rank 2, 2 additional AP. At rank 3, 3 additional AP. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkMovingTargetDescription: When you perform the Sprint action, the PC's Defense increases by +1 until the start of their next turn.
perkMysteriousSaviorDescription: At the start of a combat encounter, you may spend 1 Luck point. If you do, the first time you start dying, the GM may have a Mysterious Savior appear, stabilize you, restore you to consciousness, and grant 4 HP. If the Savior does not appear, the Luck point is refunded.
perkMysteriousStrangerDescription: From time to time, a mysterious stranger comes to the character's aid with lethal results. At the start of a combat, the player can spend 1 Luck point. Doing so, at any time during the scene, the GM can have the Mysterious Stranger appear and make a single ranged attack against an enemy the PC has attacked, or was just attacked by, and then vanish. If a Luck point is spent and the Mysterious Stranger does not appear, the GM must return the spent Luck point. The Mysterious Stranger has an AGI 10 and Small Guns 6; Small Guns is considered a tag skill. He always rolls 3d20 for attacks instead of 2d20, and his attack (using a custom .44 Magnum revolver) deals 8 Combat Dice of Vicious and Piercing 1 physical damage. He always appears within the ideal range for his weapon. Any attempt to discover where the Mysterious Stranger went after the attack fails.
perkNaturalResistanceDescription: Breathing in toxic fumes and sleeping rough on the ground no longer poses a risk of catching a disease.
perkNerdRageDescription: When the character's health falls below ¼ of maximum health, add +1 to Physical DR, +1 to Energy DR, and +1 Combat Die to the damage of all attacks. At rank 2, the values increase to +2 DR and +2 Combat Dice damage. At rank 3, the values increase to +3 DR and +3 Combat Dice damage. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkNightEyesDescription: When attempting to avoid attention or hide from an enemy, you may ignore difficulty increases caused by poor lighting or darkness.
perkNightPersonDescription: Allows reducing any difficulty increase due to darkness by 1.
perkNinjaDescription: When a sneak attack is made with a melee weapon or unarmed, damage is increased by +2 Combat Dice. You cannot receive the benefits of this perk while wearing Power Armor.
perkNocturnalFortitudeDescription: At night, your maximum HP increases by an amount equal to your Endurance. When your maximum HP increases, you gain the same amount of additional current HP. When the effect ends, your maximum HP returns to normal, but current HP is only reduced if it exceeds the new maximum.
perkNuclearPhysicistDescription: Whenever using a weapon that deals Radiation damage or has the radioactive damage effect, each Effect obtained deals an additional point of Radiation damage. Additionally, used fusion cores have 3 additional charges.
perkOverlyGenerousDescription: When you have Radiation damage equal to or greater than one-quarter of your maximum HP, your melee attacks gain the Radioactive damage effect.
perkPackMuleDescription: Your companion can carry extra weight. Increases carry capacity by 25kg.
perkPackRatDescription: Junk items you carry count as half their listed weight.
perkPainTrainDescription: The character can Charge as a major action if wearing Power Armor or if they are a super mutant. [...] If successful, the enemy suffers damage equal to the character's normal unarmed damage and falls prone. At rank 2, +1 Combat Die is added to the damage dealt and the Stun damage effect. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkPannapictagraphistDescription: When you roll randomly to obtain a magazine from the Random Publication or Issues tables, you may re-roll the result if your original result is a magazine or issue you have already found.
perkParalyzingPalmDescription: When making an unarmed attack and choosing to hit a specific point, the attack gains the Stun damage effect.
perkPartyBoyGirlDescription: The character cannot develop an addiction to alcoholic drinks and every time they drink one, they restore +2 HP.
perkPathfinderDescription: When traveling long distances through the wilderness, a successful PER + Survival check (difficulty determined by the GM based on terrain) halves the travel time.
perkPharmacistDescription: RadAway you administer heals 2 additional Radiation damage. At rank 2, it heals 3 additional Radiation damage. At rank 3, it heals 4 additional Radiation damage. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 6.
perkPhotosyntheticDescription: When in direct sunlight you regenerate 1 HP every hour. At rank 2, you regenerate 2 HP every hour in direct sunlight. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 10.
perkPickpocketDescription: At rank 1, you can ignore the first complication obtained when making an AGI + Stealth check to steal an item from someone or put something in their pocket. At rank 2, you can reroll 1d20 of the character's dice pool when attempting to pickpocket someone. At rank 3, reduce the difficulty of pickpocketing attempts by 1. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 3.
perkPiercingStrikeDescription: The character's unarmed and bladed melee weapon attacks gain the Piercing 1 damage effect, or add +1 to the value of any existing Piercing X damage effect.
perkPowerPatcherDescription: When you repair the HP of a piece of Power Armor, repair an additional 2 HP. At rank 2, 3 additional HP. At rank 3, 4 additional HP. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 10.
perkPowerUserDescription: Fusion Cores you use now contain 3 additional charges. At rank 2, 6 additional charges. At rank 3, 10 additional charges. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 10.
perkProtectorDescription: Your companion shields you from harm. Grants +1 DR to all damage types when nearby.
perkPrudentDescription: Whenever you make a skill check and purchase one or more d20s by spending Action Points, the character can reroll 1d20 on that check. You cannot purchase this perk if you already possess Daring.
perkPsychopathDescription: When you kill an enemy, roll DC equal to your rank in this perk. For each Effect rolled, regain 1 Luck point (up to your maximum). Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 6.
perkPyromaniacDescription: Damage dealt using fire-based weapons increases by +1 Combat Die per rank. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkQuackSurgeonDescription: When you perform a First Aid action on an ally, you may use an alcoholic beverage as part of the action. The patient heals additional HP equal to what the beverage normally heals, and you may heal 2 HP per AP spent. The ally also receives the benefits and drawbacks of consuming the beverage.
perkQuickDrawDescription: Each turn, the character can draw a single weapon or an item they are carrying without using a minor action.
perkQuickHandsDescription: The character is able to reload firearms faster. When making a ranged attack, the character can spend 2 AP to increase the Fire Rate of their weapon by +2 for that attack.
perkRadResistantDescription: Radiation Damage Resistance in all hit locations increases by +1 for each rank in this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkRadicoolDescription: When you have Radiation damage equal to one-quarter or more of your maximum HP, add +1 DC to melee attacks and re-roll 1d20 on STR tests. At half your max HP in Radiation, the bonuses increase to +2 DC and re-rolling 2d20. At three-quarters, +3 DC and re-rolling 3d20.
perkRefractorDescription: Energy Damage Resistance in all hit locations increases by +1 for each rank in this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkRejuvenatedDescription: When you are full (highest hunger state), your maximum HP increases by +2, you may re-roll 1d20 on all STR-based skill tests, and being full lasts twice as long. When you are quenched (highest thirst state), you may re-roll 1d20 on all END-based tests, generate one free AP at the start of each turn in combat, and being quenched lasts twice as long.
perkRepairBotDescription: Your companion can perform basic repairs. Can repair items in the field.
perkResponderDescription: When you succeed at a First Aid action in combat to stabilize a dying ally, you no longer need to spend 1 AP to wake them. The patient heals 2 HP at the start of their next turn. At rank 2, they heal 4 HP over their next two turns. At rank 3, 6 HP over their next three turns. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 7.
perkRetributionDescription: If you do not take damage from an attack due to your Damage Reduction, recover +1 HP and add 1 AP to the group pool. This effect can only trigger once per round of combat per rank in this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkRevenantDescription: When you are revived, the damage of all your attacks increases by +2 DC for the remainder of the scene, or until you are reduced to 0 HP again.
perkRicochetDescription: If an enemy makes a ranged attack against the character and rolls a complication, you can spend a Luck point to have the ricochet hit them. Then resolve the damage against the attacking enemy.
perkRiflemanDescription: When making a ranged attack with any two-handed weapon with a Fire Rate of 2 or higher (except heavy weapons), add +1 Combat Die to the weapon's damage for each rank. At rank 2, also add the Piercing 1 damage effect, or add +1 to any Piercing X damage effect already present on the weapon. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkRobotWranglerDescription: You have learned to command and control robotic companions. Unlocks the Companion tab, allowing you to recruit and manage a robot companion.
perkRoboticsExpertDescription: At rank 1, the PC can modify armor, weapon mounts, and robot modules with level 1 mods. At rank 2, they gain access to level 2 mods and the difficulty of checks to repair robots is permanently reduced by 1. At rank 3, they gain access to level 3 mods and can reprogram robots to perform a different function or alter their behavior at the GM's discretion. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkScattershotDescription: When you make a ranged attack with a shotgun, you may take an additional major action by spending 1 AP rather than 2, so long as this additional action is also a ranged attack with the same shotgun.
perkScienceDescription: You can modify energy weapons with weapon mods and you can also create advanced mods for armor. Each rank in this perk unlocks an additional level of modifications: rank 1 unlocks level 1 mods, rank 2 unlocks level 2, and so on. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkScoundrelDescription: You can ignore the first complication obtained when making a CHA + Speech check to convince someone of a lie.
perkScoutDescription: Your companion has keen senses. Grants +2 to Perception checks when scouting.
perkScrapperDescription: When the PC scraps an item, they can recover uncommon component materials in addition to common ones. At rank 2, rare materials can also be recovered. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkScroungerDescription: Whenever you roll to determine the amount of ammunition found, the PC gets a larger amount. [...] Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkSecretAgentDescription: When you use a Stealth Boy, it lasts for one additional turn. At rank 2, 2 additional turns. At rank 3, 3 additional turns. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkSerendipityDescription: When your current HP is below one-third of your maximum, whenever you are attacked you may spend 1 Luck point before any dice are rolled to have the attack miss. You may do this a number of times per scene equal to your rank in this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 6.
perkShotgunSurgeonDescription: Ranged attacks with shotguns gain the Piercing 1 damage effect, or add +1 to the value of any Piercing X damage effect already present on the weapon.
perkSizeMattersDescription: When making a ranged attack with a heavy weapon, add +1 Combat Die to the weapon's damage for each rank. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkSkilledDescription: Allows you to add +1 rank to two skills, or +2 ranks to one skill. No skill may have more than 6 ranks. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 3.
perkSlayerDescription: When dealing damage with an unarmed or melee weapon attack, the character can spend 1 Luck point to immediately deal a critical hit, and thus an injury, at the hit location.
perkSlowMetabolizerDescription: When you eat a food item, it moves your Hunger state back up towards full by one more step than normal.
perkSmoothTalkerDescription: When making a Barter or Speech check in an opposed check, you can reroll 1d20.
perkSnakeFancierDescription: The character's Poison damage resistance increases by +2.
perkSniperDescription: When you perform the Aim minor action and then execute a ranged attack with a two-handed weapon equipped with the Accurate quality, the PC can specify a hit location to strike without the attack difficulty increasing.
perkSolarPoweredDescription: For every hour spent in direct sunlight, the PC heals 1 Radiation damage.
perkSpiritualHealerDescription: When you succeed at a First Aid action to stabilize a dying ally, you regain 1 HP. At rank 2, you regain 2 HP plus 2 more at the start of your next turn. At rank 3, 3 HP plus 3 HP at the start of each of your next two turns. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 9.
perkSquadManeuversDescription: At rank 1, when travelling at a hurried pace, you can maintain that pace for one additional hour by passing a CHA + Survival test (difficulty 1), and one further hour by spending 2 AP. At rank 2, when you spend 1 AP for an additional Move action, you can also move an ally to within reach by spending another 1 AP.
perkStabilizedDescription: When wearing Power Armor, you may re-roll 1d20 on any attack made with the Big Guns skill, and any big gun you wield gains Piercing 1 (or adds +1 to any existing Piercing X).
perkSteadyAimDescription: When performing the Aim minor action, the PC can reroll 2d20 on the first attack made that turn, or reroll 1d20 on attacks made that turn.
perkStormChaserDescription: When it is raining or during a rad storm, you regain 1 HP at the start of each of your turns in combat. Out of combat, you regain HP equal to half your Luck score (rounded up) every hour of rain.
perkStrongBackDescription: Maximum carry weight is increased by +12.5 kg per rank. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 2.
perkSturdyFrameDescription: Armor you wear—except Power Armor—counts as three-quarters of its listed weight. At rank 2, it counts as half. At rank 3, one-quarter. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 8.
perkSuperDuperDescription: Whenever you craft an item, roll 1 DC per rank. If you roll Effects, regain half the components spent for each rarity (rounded down). Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkSuppressorDescription: When you successfully attack an enemy, you may spend 1 AP to suppress them until the end of their next turn. A suppressed enemy reduces their damage by 1 DC. At rank 2, by 2 DC. At rank 3, by 3 DC. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 7.
perkTagDescription: The character possesses an additional Tag skill. Increase the skill rank by 2, up to a maximum of 6, and mark it as a Tag skill, which allows for a critical success with a d20 result equal to or lower than the skill rank.
perkTakingOneForTheTeamDescription: At rank 1, whenever a creature NPC would damage an ally in your zone, roll 1 DC: on any result other than Effect, you take the damage instead (once per round per rank). At rank 2, if you take the damage, add 1 AP per rank to the group pool. At rank 3, after taking the damage, you and the ally may each re-roll 1d20 per rank on the next attack against that enemy. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkTenderizerDescription: When you hit an enemy with a melee or ranged attack, you may spend 1 AP to make them vulnerable until the end of their next turn. A vulnerable enemy receives +1 DC additional damage. At rank 2, +2 DC. At rank 3, +3 DC. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 10.
perkTerrifyingPresenceDescription: When making a Speech check to threaten or frighten someone, you can reroll 1d20. At rank 2, you can use a major action in combat to threaten an enemy at Medium range, with a STR + Speech check at difficulty 2.
