If successful, that enemy must move away from the character during their next turn (although they can perform any other action they wish). Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 5.
perkThirstQuencherDescription: Drinking dirty water no longer poses a risk of catching a disease.
perkTinkererDescription: You reduce the difficulty of repairing a robot via First Aid to the number of injuries it has sustained (+1 if repairing yourself). You can also spend up to an hour tinkering with a robot, increasing its maximum HP by +2 for 24 hours or until it sustains an injury.
perkToughnessDescription: Physical Damage Resistance in all hit locations increases by +1 for each rank in this perk. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkTrueFriendsDescription: At rank 1, when a reputation test would decrease your reputation with a faction or settlement, roll DC equal to your current reputation: on at least one Effect, your reputation does not change. At rank 2, when increasing affinity, roll CHA + Speech (difficulty 3): on success, increase by 2 instead of 1. Each time this perk is selected, the level requirements increase by 4.
perkVaccinatedDescription: Taking damage from the claws or bite of a mammal, lizard, or insect no longer poses a risk of catching a disease.
qualityAccurateDescription: If you take the Aim minor action before attacking with an Accurate weapon, you may spend up to 3 AP to add +1 DC per AP spent to the attack's damage. If you gain damage in this way, you may not spend ammunition for extra damage. A weapon cannot be both Accurate and Inaccurate.
qualityAmmoHungryDescription: The weapon consumes ammo at a much higher rate than other weapons. Each time the weapon is fired, it spends X ammo, rather than 1. In addition, extra damage requires spending X ammo per +1 DC , with a maximum number of DC equal to the weapon’s Fire Rate. Additional uses of ammunition, such as the Burst damage effect, also spend X ammo for every 1 ammo that would normally be spent.
qualityBlastDescription: When you make an attack with a Blast weapon, you do not target a single opponent. Instead, select a single zone you can see, and make the appropriate skill test to attack, with a basic difficulty of 2 (adjusted for range as normal). If you succeed, every creature (and other damageable target) in that zone suffers the weapon's damage. If you fail, your misplaced attack is less effective: roll only half the weapon's DC to determine the damage inflicted to creatures in the target zone and ignore the weapon's normal damage effects.
qualityBombardDescription: The weapon can deliver a series of explosions over a wide area. This functions identically to the Blast quality, with one addition: When an attack is made with the weapon, you may spend additional ammunition to target additional zones. Each additional zone requires that 2 ammunition be spent, and additional zones must be adjacent to the initial target zone. Ammunition spent to target additional zones is limited by the weapon’s Fire Rate.
qualityCloseQuartersDescription: A Close Quarters weapon is easy to use up-close, and suffers no difficulty increase for being used when within Reach of an enemy.
qualityConcealedDescription: A Concealed weapon is small, or otherwise easy to hide on your person. Enemies do not spot a Concealed weapon unless you're wielding it, or if they make a thorough search and succeed at a PER + Survival test with a difficulty of 2.
qualityCripplingDescription: The difficulty of any test to heal injuries caused by a Crippling weapon is increased by 1.
qualityDebilitatingDescription: The difficulty of any skill test to treat injuries inflicted by a Debilitating weapon increase by +1.
qualityDelayDescription: The weapon operates on a timer or fuse, and will not detonate immediately. A Thrown Delay weapon can be thrown normally to the target location, while other Delay weapons may be placed in their desired location. A Delay (1) weapon detonates at the start of the first round after it is thrown or otherwise triggered. A Delay (2) weapon detonates at the start of the round after that, and so on.
qualityGatlingDescription: Ammunition is spent at ten times the normal rate by Gatling weapons: whenever you would spend one shot of ammunition, a Gatling weapon instead spends a burst of 10 shots. Whenever you spend ammunition to increase this weapon's damage, add +2 DC per ten-shot burst (to a maximum number of bursts equal to the weapon's Fire Rate), rather than +1 DC per shot.
qualityInaccurateDescription: When making an attack with an Inaccurate weapon, you gain no benefit from the Aim minor action. A weapon may not be both Accurate and Inaccurate.
qualityMeleeDescription: Can be used as a melee weapon, inflicting 4 DC Physical Piercing damage
qualityMineDescription: When a Mine is placed onto a surface and primed, it becomes a dangerous object, inflicting its damage upon anyone who comes within Reach of it (and upon additional characters, if it has the Blast quality).
qualityMrHandyOnlyDescription: This weapon is integrated into a Mr Handy robot's body and can only be used by Mr Handy units. It cannot be equipped by other character types.
qualityNightVisionDescription: The sights of a weapon with Night Vision have been made to allow you to see more clearly in the dark. When you Aim with a Night Vision weapon, you ignore any increase in the difficulty of an attack due to darkness.
qualityParryDescription: When an enemy attempts a melee attack against you, and you are wielding a Parry weapon, you may spend 1 AP to add +1 to your Defense against that attack.
qualityPlacedDescription: The explosive device is placed in a specific location and set to detonate, often either with the Delay quality or the Remote quality. Placing the explosive requires a PER + Explosives test with a difficulty of 1; this increases to 2 if you’ve been attacked since your last turn. Failure means the bomb hasn’t been set properly and will not detonate (or, if a complication is rolled, may have been set improperly, and may detonate unexpectedly). Success means the bomb has been set up properly.
qualityRecoilDescription: This weapon has a particularly ferocious ‘kick’ when it fires, which might be too much for weaker users. If the user’s STR score is less than X, then all attacks with the weapon increase in difficulty by 1. You may ignore this effect if you wield the weapon two-handed, or if the weapon is already two-handed, by bracing it against a solid object or a bipod.
qualityReconDescription: When you Aim with a Recon weapon, you may mark the target you aimed at. The next ally to attack that target may re-roll one d20 on their attack.
qualityReliableDescription: During each combat encounter, a Reliable weapon ignores the first complication you roll on a test to use that weapon. A weapon may not be both Reliable and Unreliable.
qualitySlowLoadDescription: The weapon loads especially slowly. After this weapon has been fired, you must spend a minor action to reload the weapon before it can be fired again.
qualitySuppressedDescription: If an enemy is not aware of you when you attack with a Suppressed weapon, they do not notice the attack unless they are the target or they pass a PER + Survival test with a difficulty of 2.
qualitySurgeDescription: The weapon is especially damaging to machines like robots and powered Armor. The weapon gains the Vicious damage effect against Robots, enemies in Power Armor, and other mechanical or electronic targets (like turrets).
qualityThrownDescription: A Thrown (C) weapon can be thrown, as a ranged attack with an ideal range of Close. A Thrown (M) weapon can be thrown, as a ranged attack with an ideal range of Medium. You make an AGI + Throwing test to attack with the weapon.
qualityTwoHandedDescription: A Two-Handed weapon must be held in two hands to be used effectively; attempting to attack with a Two-Handed weapon in one hand increases the difficulty by +2.
qualityUnreliableDescription: When you make an attack with an Unreliable weapon, increase the complication range of the attack by 1. A weapon may not be both Reliable and Unreliable.
replenishLuckAlert: Do you really want to replenish luck?
robotPartArmsDescription: The robot's arms are the manipulator limbs that allow it to interact with the environment, carry objects, and use tools or weapons. They can be equipped with various modifications to improve performance.
robotPartBodyDescription: The body is the robot's central structure, containing the main systems and providing structural support. It can be modified with different plating to increase resistance or storage capacity.
robotPartSensorsDescription: The sensors are the robot's optical and perceptual system, allowing it to see, hear, and detect the surrounding environment. They can be equipped with various types of plating and modifications to improve detection capabilities.
robotPartThrustersDescription: The thrusters are the robot's aerial locomotion system, allowing it to fly and move quickly. They can be modified with different plating to improve speed or resistance.
traitBrahminBaronBenefit: Each Brahmin Feed Trough provides upkeep for 3 Brahmin in each of your settlements. When you perform the Tend Crops settlement action, you gain an additional +1 DC Brahmin milk per feed trough.
traitBrahminBaronPenalty: Roll +1d20 for determining the risk of attack to your settlement if its Food resource exceeds the settlement's People (see p.39).
traitBrotherhoodChainDescription: As a member of the Brotherhood of Steel, you are bound to the chain of command. You must execute the orders of your direct superiors and are responsible for your subordinate brothers. If you fail in your duty, you will be expelled from the Brotherhood and the technology in your possession will be recovered, by any means necessary.
traitEducatedBenefit: The character has an additional tag skill.
traitEducatedPenalty: When failing a skill test using a skill other than a tag skill, the GM gains 1 AP.
traitExtraPerkBenefit: The character gains an additional perk at level 1.
traitExtraPerkPenalty: The character can choose only 1 trait instead of 2.
traitFastShotBenefit: If performing a second major action in combat to make a ranged attack, the cost of that additional major action will be 1 AP instead of 2.
traitFastShotPenalty: Cannot benefit from the Aim minor action during this frantic action.
traitForcedEvolutionDescription:
Your initial Strength and Endurance attributes are increased by +2 each, and your maximum Strength and Endurance are increased to 12, but your maximum Intelligence and Charisma are both reduced to 6. You may not have more than 4 ranks in any skill. You are completely immune to radiation and poison damage.
You stand over seven feet tall, and your body is bulky and muscular. Your skin is green, yellow, or grey, regardless of what color it was when you were human. You do not seem to age, but you are sterile. You can only wear armor which has been made to fit a super mutant.
traitGhoulAgelessDescription: You age at a greatly reduced rate and are likely older than your non-mutated companions (you may have even survived the Great War of 2077), but you are sterile.
traitGhoulDiscriminationDescription: You may suffer discrimination from 'smoothskins' (humans who are not ghouls), causing an increase in difficulty or complication range of Charisma tests depending on the opponent's beliefs.
traitGhoulRadiationHealingDescription: You are healed by radiation: recover 1 HP for every 3 points of Radiation damage taken.
traitGhoulRadiationRestDescription: If you rest in an irradiated location, you can reroll the dice pool when making a test to see if wounds heal.
traitGiftedBenefit: Choose two S.P.E.C.I.A.L. attributes and increase them by +1 each.
traitGiftedPenalty: The maximum number of Luck Points equals the character's Luck attribute, minus one.
traitGoodNaturedBenefit: Tag two of either Speech, Medicine, Repair, Science, and Barter skills.
traitGoodNaturedPenalty: The maximum rating for the other skills listed is reduced from 6 to 4.
traitGruntBenefit: +1 DC damage with submachine guns, combat rifles, assault rifles, frag grenades, and combat knives.
traitGruntPenalty: The complication range of tests while making an attack with big guns or energy weapons is increased by 2.
traitHeavyHandedBenefit: The Melee Damage bonus increases by +1 DC.
traitHeavyHandedPenalty: Melee and unarmed attacks suffer a complication on a 19 or 20, instead of only on a 20.
traitHomeOnTheRangeBenefit: Whenever you sleep by a campfire for at least 6 hours, the difficulty to recover from injuries through rest is decreased by 1.
traitHomeOnTheRangePenalty: You cannot gain a well rested bonus, as described in Fallout: The Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook p.35.
traitMotherWastelandBenefit: Spend 1 Luck point to gain insight into the quest, situation, or scene as if you'd spent 3 AP to use Obtain Information 3 times. This information is often cryptic and mystical in nature.
traitMotherWastelandPenalty: Complications make scenes involving pre-War artifacts harder to understand, limiting your use of them.
traitMrHandyAutomaticPistolBenefit: Can make ranged attacks using a 10mm automatic pistol (see 10mm Pistol with automatic receiver mod, p. 95).
traitMrHandyCircularSawBenefit: Allows cutting objects and making melee attacks with the circular saw. The circular saw deals 3 DC Physical Piercing 1 damage.
traitMrHandyDescription:
You have 360° vision and improved sensory systems that can detect smells, chemicals, and radiation, reducing the difficulty of Perception tests that rely on sight and smell by
1. You are also immune to radiation and poison damage, but you cannot use chems, nor can you benefit from food, drink, or rest. You move by jet propulsion, hovering above the ground, unaffected by difficult terrain or obstacles. You carry weight is 150 Ibs., and it cannot be increased by your Strength or perks, but it can be increased by modified armor. You cannot recover from your own injuries or heal health points without receiving repairs.
traitMrHandyFlamethrowerBenefit: Allows igniting objects, cooking food, or making ranged attacks with a short-range flamethrower (see flamethrower, p. 106).
traitMrHandyLaserEmitterBenefit: Allows cutting objects or making ranged attacks using a laser (see laser pistol, p. 101).
traitMrHandyPliersBenefit: Allows picking up and carrying objects weighing no more than 20 kg and manipulating surrounding objects. Can also make unarmed attacks (2 DC Physical damage) with the pliers.
traitNomadBenefit: Re-roll 1d20 on Survival tests to travel, set up camp, and forage for food and water. In addition, ignore the first complication rolled when making these tests.
traitNomadPenalty: Barter and Speech tests increase their difficulty and complication range by 1 when dealing with inhabitants of a static settlement. Science cannot be a Tag skill.
traitRiteOfPassageBenefit: The first time you spend Luck in a scene, roll 1 DC. If you roll an effect you have not spent that Luck point.
traitRiteOfPassagePenalty: You view everyone who hasn't gone through your rite of passage as lesser. You cannot assist another PC without spending 1 AP first.
traitSmallFrameBenefit: Can reroll 1d20 on all AGI tests based on balance or contortion.
traitSmallFramePenalty: Carry weight is 75 + (2.5 × STR) kg, instead of 75 + (5 × STR) kg.
traitTheChosenOneBenefit: The first d20 you purchase is free on tests relating to your tribe's quest, and you can always succeed at a cost.
traitTheChosenOnePenalty: The GM adds 2 AP to their pool when your quest comes up.
traitToolsOfTheOldWorldBenefit: You may use Survival instead of Repair or Science to repair or make use of pre-War tech.
traitToolsOfTheOldWorldPenalty: The complication range of tests involving pre-War technology (not weapons) is increased by 2.
traitTriggerDisciplineBenefit: When you make ranged attacks using small guns or energy weapons you may re-roll 1d20.
traitTriggerDisciplinePenalty: While you wield small guns and energy weapons, reduce their fire rate by 1.
traitVaultKidDescription:
Your healthier start to life at the hands of trained doctors and sophisticated auto-docs means you reduce the difficulty of all END tests to resist the effects of disease. In addition, your carefully-planned upbringing means you have one additional tag skill of your choice.
You may also work with the Gamemaster to determine what sort of experiment took place within your Vault. Once per quest, the GM may introduce a complication which reflects the nature of the experiment you unwittingly took part in, or introduce a complication related to your early life of isolation and confinement within the Vault. If the GM does this, you immediately regain one Luck Point.
weapon127mmPistolDescription: A heavy-duty single-shot weapon with exceptional stopping power. Most known examples are believed to have been imported from overseas before the war. Given the weapon’s punishing recoil, it can be tricky to use for those not prepared for its kick.
weapon127mmSmgDescription: An uncommon mixture of compact weapon and large-caliber round, the 12.7mm submachine gun features an unconventional top-mounted magazine, lying parallel to the weapon’s barrel. While small and lightweight itself, the weapon’s recoil and rate of fire can make it tricky to handle.
weapon25mmGrenadeApwDescription: The 25mm grenade APW is a semi-automatic grenade launcher. Specifically, this weapon contains a stock magazine capacity of six grenades, a brisk rate of fire, decent accuracy and allows modifications. While the grenades are much smaller and less impactful than those found in many other grenade launchers, the weapon itself is not as cumbersome as larger grenade launchers.
weapon357RevolverDescription: A single-action revolver chambered for .357 magnum (though ones using .38 special rounds also exist). Not as potent as the feared and respected .44 revolver, these guns remain scattered around parts of the Wasteland due to their common use by police forces as service weapons and due to their extremely simple, durable build that makes them easy to maintain and extremely reliable.
weapon50CalMachineGunDescription: These machine guns, chambered for the powerful .50 round, are extremely effective against infantry, materiel, lightly armored vehicles, and a wide range of other targets. This design has been in use since long before the Great War but finds a new lease of life when carried by Power Armored fighters.
weapon9mmPistolDescription: A common sight in the California and Mojave Wastelands and used as a standard sidearm by the New California Republic Army, this model of 9mm autoloader handgun was manufactured in vast quantities before the Great War. Its light weight, ease of handling, and common ammunition makes it a popular choice for personal defense, or as a backup weapon. However, the sturdy design doesn’t lend itself as well to modifications as other common weapons.
weaponAlienAtomizerDescription: A form of alien energy pistol, found in use by the extra-terrestrial beings sometimes called “Zetans”. Encounters with these creatures are extremely rare, and their weapons are extremely potent. Nobody understands their technology well enough to modify these guns, however.
weaponAlienDisintegratorDescription: A form of alien energy rifle, appearing like a space-age blunderbuss, found in use by the extra-terrestrial beings sometimes called “Zetans”. Like the smaller atomizers, the technology of these weapons is too unknown for anyone to successfully modify one of these weapons.
weaponAluminumBaseballBatDescription: Baseball bats were once used in the sport of baseball, considered America's number-one pastime during the pre-War era. Two hundred years later, they have been re-purposed as crude but effective melee weapons. While most are made a single piece of wood—normally ash, but other hardwoods are often used—a few can be found made of aluminum instead, making for a lighter, harder bat.
weaponAntiMaterielRifleDescription: Utilizing an ergonomic, metallic skeleton design with a free-floating barrel, the anti-materiel rifle is a bolt-action, high-caliber precision weapon intended for use against materiel and hard targets. The weapon is designed for use at long ranges and should ideally not be fired while standing up due to the heavy recoil. Rare and highly sought-after, these guns are ideal for dealing with things like deathclaws, the larger radscorpions, and other sizeable threats… so long as you’ve got time to set up the first shot.
weaponArcWelderDescription: This odd weapon can discharge a constant, high-voltage stream of electricity. While painful and damaging to living creatures, it is especially effective against robots and enemies in Power Armor, as electricity discharges into delicate components.
weaponAssaultRifleDescription: This gas-operated rifle is a common sight across the wasteland, and well-liked because of the low recoil and reasonable accuracy, and the ease of modifications. Most versions found are only capable of semi-automatic fire, but the weapon is easily modified for automatic fire.
weaponAssaultronBladeDescription: The vibroblade from an Assaultron dominator, dismounted and repurposed into a usable melee weapon.
weaponAssaultronHeadDescription: The head of an Assaultron contains a high-yield laser emitter, shielded behind sturdy Armor plating. Assaultrons use this laser to blast the enemy as they close into melee. The laser itself uses a capacitor that can build up an extremely powerful charge, but which requires considerable energy for each fully charged blast.
weaponAtomicMineDescription: A nuclear landmine. Functions like any other mine, but its explosion resembles that of a MiniNuke.
weaponAutoAxeDescription: Sharing some basic qualities with the Ripper but more impressive and harmful, the auto axe is a steel saw fitted with car engine parts to increase its attack damage. The original blade has been replaced or modified to resemble four axe blades arranged around the central axle.
weaponAutoGrenadeLauncherDescription: Sometimes referred to as a grenade machine gun, this is a belt-fed automatic weapon designed to lay down a heavy volume of explosive ordnance. Since the advent of Power Armor, many units were converted for use on foot rather than from a tripod.
weaponBallisticFistDescription: The ballistic fist consists of a wrist-mounted shotgun with a pressure plate worn over the knuckles. The impact of the plate triggers the firing mechanism when a punch connects with the target, firing the shotgun directly into the target. This weapon requires ammunition, expending one shotgun shell with each attack.
weaponBaseballBatDescription: Baseball bats were once used in the sport of baseball, considered America's number-one pastime during the pre-War era. Two hundred years later, they have been re-purposed as crude but effective melee weapons. While most are made a single piece of wood—normally ash, but other hardwoods are often used—a few can be found made of aluminum instead, making for a lighter, harder bat.
weaponBatonDescription: A collapsible baton of the sort used by police departments and private security forces before the Great War. Vault-Tec security within each vault had a stock of these, so they remain in common use as a lightweight back-up melee weapon.
weaponBattleRifleDescription: Something of a relic of old 20th-century engineering, this sturdy, gas-operated semi-automatic rifle is nevertheless a solid choice of rifle for the discerning raider or mercenary. Its old-fashioned construction makes for a durable weapon with considerable stopping power, but it isn’t easily modified.
weaponBayonetDescription: A blade fixed to the muzzle of a firearm, used as a melee weapon in close combat.
weaponBlackPowderBlunderbussDescription: An archaic percussion-cap firearm, of a kind possibly salvaged from museums or the collections of reenactors. They fire a heavy .50 musket ball propelled by gunpowder, and are only effective at very close ranges, but when they do hit, they can do a lot of damage.
weaponBlackPowderPistolDescription: An archaic percussion-cap firearm, of a kind possibly salvaged from museums or the collections of reenactors. Compared to the blunderbuss, the pistol is less inaccurate and has a slightly longer effective range, but it packs no less of a punch.
weaponBlackPowderRifleDescription: An archaic percussion-cap muzzle-loading rifle, of a kind possibly salvaged from museums or the collections of reenactors. The rifle’s longer barrel gives it considerably longer range and slightly more stopping power than its smaller counterparts, but while it’s fine against one or two foes, the slow rate of fire and long reload process make it inconvenient when heavily outnumbered.
weaponBoardDescription: Little more than a long plank of wood with a handle wrapped in duct tape. Boards are heavy melee weapons, normally favored by super mutants who have the strength to wield them effectively.
weaponBottlecapMineDescription: A crudely assembled mine packed with bottlecaps and a rough explosive mixture.
weaponBoxingGloveDescription: A large, leather mitt, used to protect the hands and wrists while punching. While originally used during sporting matches, a skilled fighter can use these to deliver solid blows to enemies in less organized fights, particularly if they've modified the glove in some way.
weaponBumperSwordDescription: The bumper sword consists of a chrome car bumper that has been pounded out and sharpened into a thick, heavy blade. The license plate is still attached, and an exhaust pipe strapped to the unsharpened end serves as a makeshift handle.
weaponCattleProdDescription: Normally used for herding stubborn cattle, it has been repurposed as a weapon against humans. Outfitted with a thick, electrified metal coil around the striking end, hooked up to batteries taped near the handle.
weaponChainsawDescription: A motorized tool—traditionally used for cutting wood—with a toothed metal chain propelled around the edge of the blade. They serve as deadly weapons in the right hands. Note: A Chainsaw does not benefit from the wielder’s normal melee damage bonus.
weaponCombatKnifeDescription: Soldiers (and those who want to emulate them) often carry a knife that serves both for combat and everyday use. Most Combat Knives found in the wasteland are the same type issued to U.S. Army soldiers during the Great War, with a single-edged clip-point blade. It's a very light and durable weapon, made to be used often.
weaponCombatRifleDescription: A pre-War weapon found across the wastelands and used in large numbers by various aggressive factions. Combat Rifles are a rugged and adaptable design, able to be modified to fill a variety of different battlefield roles.
weaponCombatShotgunDescription: Combat shotguns are pump-action shotguns normally used by a military faction for an offensive purpose: they're perfect for close-quarters fighting in tunnels, inside buildings, or amongst the ruined streets of old cities.
weaponCompanionLaserDescription: An energy weapon used by robotic companions.
weaponCryoMineDescription: Cryo mines function similarly to cryogenic grenades, spraying the vicinity in freezing chemicals when the mine is triggered.
weaponCryogenicGrenadeDescription: Cryogenic grenades release a spray of aerosolized chemicals of extremely low temperature—liquid nitrogen is a common ingredient—which rapidly saps the heat from the surrounding area, often leaving enemies and objects in the blast frozen.
weaponDeathTamboDescription: Somewhere between a set of knuckledusters and a tambourine, this weapon is an odd assortment of bells and blades designed to be punched into the enemy. Note: When used to make a sneak attack, it increases the complication range by 1.
weaponDeathclawGauntletDescription: Made from the severed fingers and talons of a deathclaw and mounted on a cuff which fits over the wrist, these allow the wearer to strike with some of the lethal force of the claws' previous owner.
weaponDetonatorDescription: Appearing as something like a pistol grip with a trigger and a short antenna, a detonator allows placed explosives to be triggered remotely. It removes the Delay (X) Trait and allows detonation as a minor action within Long range.
weaponDisplacerGloveDescription: An advanced piece of tech to accomplish a simple task. The speaker on the knuckles creates a sonic blast to add some extra impact to your punches.
weaponDoubleBarrelShotgunDescription: Before the Great War, double-barrel shotguns were used primarily for hunting and home defense… and things haven't changed much. Break-action weapons, they can be found in both side-by-side and over-under configurations. Simple to build and maintain, they're not an uncommon sight in the Commonwealth and the wider wasteland.
weaponDroneCannonDescription: Found on the arms of alien guardian drones, these strange energy weapons fire a ball of energy that acts a little like a grenade, bouncing for a few seconds before exploding.
weaponDynamiteBundleDescription: A collection of dynamite sticks bundled together with a timer as a placed bomb. Dynamite bundles have the Delay (X) quality, with the rating set when the bomb is placed.
weaponDynamiteDescription: A simple explosive, dynamite is a pressed stick of absorbent clay or similar material soaked in nitroglycerine. Sticks of dynamite are a compact and relatively cheap and stable form of explosive that isn’t too difficult to make.
weaponExplosiveBaseballDescription: A hollowed baseball packed with improvised explosives. Crude, old-fashioned, and effective.
weaponFatManDescription: The M42 Nuclear Catapult is a shoulder-fired infantry support weapon which launches compact tactical nuclear warheads—mini-nukes, each about the size of a football—over a moderate distance to inflict massive destruction upon the target location.
weaponFlamethrowerDescription: A flamethrower, or flamer, is a weapon which sprays an ignited fuel mixture over an area, essentially creating a jet of flame that can burn or ignite targets at a distance. Ideal for clearing dead foliage, irritating vermin, and fortified bunkers!
weaponFlareGunDescription: A Flare gun is a break-action single-shot device not really designed to inflict damage. Rather, it shoots signal flares which are used by many factions to signal for assistance by firing them high into the air.
weaponFlashBangDescription: These non-lethal grenades are designed to disorient enemies rather than inflict direct harm: when they detonate, they produce a blinding flash and a deafening bang which leaves enemies reeling and vulnerable (Stunned).
weaponFortyFourPistolDescription: The .44 pistol is a double-action revolver, chambered to use the .44 Magnum cartridge. It's a powerful gun, though not as customizable as other handguns. Due to their simple, rugged design, they're prized by their owners, but not so uncommon as to be truly rare.
weaponFragGrenadeMirvDescription: Fragmentation grenade MIRVs are explosive grenades that release a collection of smaller bomblets, scattering them over a wide area before they explode.
weaponFragmentationGrenadeDescription: A standard fragmentation grenade, designed to shatter into deadly shards on detonation.
weaponFragmentationMineDescription: A pressure-plate mine similar in design and construction to a fragmentation grenade.
weaponGammaPistolDescription: A crude-looking weapon, the gamma gun emits directed blasts of high-intensity ionizing radiation which can be devastating to any creature not immune nor massively resistant to radiation, while leaving other targets largely unharmed.
weaponGatlingGunDescription: Resembling an old-fashioned version of a minigun, the rotating barrels are operated by a hand-crank rather than an electric motor. It has a slower rate of fire than its advanced counterpart, but is no less functional.
weaponGatlingLaserDescription: A high rate-of-fire laser weapon, the Gatling laser uses several rotating barrels fired in quick succession to produce a devastating fusillade of laser pulses. While the individual laser pulses lack in stopping power, the sheer mass of them can wear down almost any target.
weaponGatlingPlasmaDescription: An evolution of plasma technology, it inflicts damage through a combination of physical impact and thermal transfer. It consumes ammunition at a rapid rate, making supply a full-time job. Note: Inflicts both Physical and Energy damage (use the lower resistance).
weaponGaussMinigunDescription: A powerful, automatic ballistic weapon that rapidly fires hypervelocity 2mm rounds. It is a prestigious weapon that demands a wealthy owner due to its rare ammunition and sheer rate of fire.
weaponGaussPistolDescription: A handheld alternative to a full-sized gauss rifle. Despite its small size, it maintains formidable stopping power over considerable distances, and in the hands of a skilled user, it can bring down even the most durable foes.
weaponGaussRifleDescription: The gauss rifle uses magnetic induction to propel a projectile at incredible, devastating speeds. Each shot can be 'charged' for maximum damage by holding the trigger for a moment before releasing to fire.
weaponGaussShotgunDescription: Technically more of a volley gun than a shotgun, as it fires multiple shots from multiple barrels at once, the gauss shotgun is a rare and devastating weapon which peppers a target with hypervelocity rounds.
weaponGuitarSwordDescription: A sword shaped like a guitar, or a guitar that doubles as a sword. Note: When used to make a sneak attack, it increases the complication range by 1 due to the sound it makes.
weaponHandmadeBoltActionDescription: A makeshift weapon, easily spotted in the hands of those who can't build, buy, or scavenge anything better. Pipe weapons are crude and somewhat unreliable, but effective enough; bolt-action pipe rifles are normally made to fire rifle rounds, sacrificing fire rate for power and simplicity.
weaponHandmadePistolDescription: Primitive, homemade, normally wielded by those who can't build, buy, or scavenge anything better, pipe pistols are crude and somewhat unreliable, but effective enough. The most common resemble small semi-automatic pistols, made to take common .38 rounds… though there are known to be those bold (or desperate) enough to modify them for automatic or precision fire.
weaponHandmadeRevolverDescription: Like other pipe weapons, these are makeshift or homemade pistols, in this case based on a revolver. They're a middle ground between the firepower of a bolt-action and the rate-of-fire of a pistol, and being a pipe weapon, they're similarly easy to modify.
weaponHuntingRifleDescription: A bolt-action rifle commonly found in the hands of super mutants and raiders. The simple design made them common amongst hunters and survivalists before the Great War, and they're endlessly customizable. It seems fanciful to suggest that the simple short-barreled slug-thrower carried by a super mutant could be turned into a precision sniper's rifle, yet that describes the hunting rifle perfectly.
weaponIncineratorDescription: A heavy incinerator sprays an ignited fuel mixture over the target to burn them, launching a series of fireballs that explode on impact and ignite as the fuel splashes the target.
weaponInstituteLaserDescription: Developed by The Institute after the Great War, Institute lasers differ in design and function from the pre-War lasers used by other factions such as the Brotherhood of Steel. Eschewing raw power for a rapid succession of beams, these lasers can lay down a searing volley of suppressive fire.
weaponIronFistDescription: A powerful strike with bare hands.
weaponJavelinDescription: A short throwing spear, not sturdy enough for melee but accurate enough to be thrown at medium range.
weaponJunkJetDescription: The Junk Jet is a makeshift heavy weapon which fires anything. Any items loaded into the weapon's hopper are hurled with deadly force from the business end of the gun. Naturally, this makes it quite useful in situations where random junk is abundant but actual ammunition is scarce.
weaponKnucklesDescription: Developed in antiquity, this weapon is nevertheless quite prevalent in the post-Great War wastelands. Brass Knuckles, or Knuckledusters, take the form of four linked metal rings in a shallow convex formation with a bumper attached to the concave face. The rings are slipped over a combatant's fingers with the bumper pressed into the palm of the hand. When a punch is thrown, the Brass Knuckles take most of the impact impulse, transferring much of the punch's kinetic energy to the target by way of the harder, smaller impacting surface of the metal knuckles. This drastically increases the likelihood of serious tissue disruption and bone damage.
weaponLaserMusketDescription: The laser musket is a homemade form of laser rifle, blending advanced technology with strangely primitive mechanisms, popular with militia groups such as the Minutemen. The laser musket must be manually primed, charging the makeshift capacitor through turning a hand-crank on the weapon's rear. While seemingly crude, they can make for potent sniper's weapons, especially with a modified capacitor able to hold a larger charge.
weaponLaserPistolDescription: Laser guns are high-tech weapons which emit a concentrated beam of coherent, directional light, intense enough to inflict serious damage. They were a relatively new development in the years before the Great War, and thus are not an especially common sight in the wasteland, outside of those groups who've secured old military weapons caches. Most surviving models of laser gun—such as the dependable AER9, widely used by the U. S. Army—use an internal capacitor charged by a microfusion power cell.
weaponLeadPipeDescription: A length of heavy, metal (normally lead) pipe, with a duct-tape wrapped grip at one end and a few nuts and bolts attached at the other. Crude, simple, and hurts like hell.
weaponLeverActionRifleDescription: As the name suggests, a lever-action rifle uses a lever to cycle the weapon’s action; this is typically part of the trigger guard, allowing the shooter to cycle the action with minimal effort or movement. Though this slows the rate of fire, it’s a reliable design that stands up to use, time, and nuclear war.
weaponLightMachineGunDescription: The light machine gun is built to provide heavy firepower in a package not much larger than a typical rifle. While certainly heavier and more cumbersome than an assault rifle, it maintains a high rate of fire and can be easily modified with parts used by a variety of similar weapons, allowing the user to customize their fire support.
weaponMacheteDescription: A long, sharp knife designed for cutting through dense vegetation, which makes it both a very useful item and an effective weapon. Some of those found in the wasteland are true machetes, while others are makeshift weapons, typically consisting of a sharpened metal blade (taken from a lawnmower, for example) that is nailed, tied, glued, or otherwise attached to a simple handle.
weaponMachineGunDescription: Submachine guns were an old design, even before the War, and while they're hideously inaccurate, their rate-of-fire means you don't need to be accurate.
weaponMicrowaveEmitterDescription: This advanced energy weapon generates intense blasts of microwave radiation. While it won’t irradiate you—microwaves are non-ionizing radiation—it can cause intense burns as whatever is hit by the blast is heated up from the inside, boiling the water in your cells. Metal is little protection: the microwave blast can’t effectively be dispersed by metallic objects.
weaponMinigunDescription: The minigun is a motorized Gatling-style rapid fire weapon. Miniguns can be found as mounted door guns on Vertibirds or as a handheld heavy assault and support weapon. The high rate-of-fire of the weapon means that it can produce devastating results, but at a cost of consuming ammunition at a prodigious rate.
weaponMissileLauncherBayonetDescription: A bayonet attached to a missile launcher, used as a melee weapon in close combat.
weaponMissileLauncherDescription: A refined, highly adaptable multipurpose missile launcher. This modular launcher is smaller and more maneuverable than earlier models, consisting of a two-part launch tube, firing mechanism, mounting brackets, and sighting apparatus.
weaponMolotovCocktailDescription: A glass bottle filled with flammable liquid and a cloth wick. Light and throw at an enemy to create a burst of fire.
weaponMrHandyAutomaticPistolDescription: Automatic 10mm pistol integrated into a Mr. Handy's body. Only Mr. Handy can use this weapon.
weaponMrHandyBuzzBladeDescription: The buzzsaw arm of a Mister Handy, given new life attached to a small motor and a long haft, such as from a broom or a weed whacker.
weaponMrHandyCircularSawDescription: Circular saw integrated into a Mr. Handy's body. High-speed rotating blade that can cut through hard materials. Only Mr. Handy can use this weapon.
weaponMrHandyFlamethrowerDescription: Flamethrower integrated into a Mr. Handy's body. Sprays an ignited fuel mixture over an area, essentially creating a jet of flame that can burn or ignite targets at a distance. Only Mr. Handy can use this weapon.
weaponMrHandyLaserEmitterDescription: Laser emitter integrated into a Mr. Handy's body. Identical to a standard laser pistol but permanently mounted on the robot. Only Mr. Handy can use this weapon.
weaponMrHandyPliersDescription: Pliers integrated into a Mr. Handy's body. Mechanical pincer used for gripping and striking. Only Mr. Handy can use this weapon.
weaponMultiPurposeAxeDescription: A pre-War tool, the Multi-Purpose Axe is equally suited to chopping lumber or splitting heads, just make sure you’re on the right end of the swing.
weaponNailGunDescription: A makeshift weapon created by enterprising wasteland engineers, the nail gun uses high-pressure steam to fire railroad spikes at a velocity sufficient to pierce and impale most targets.
weaponNukaGrenadeDescription: A makeshift nuclear grenade crafted from a Nuka-Cola bottle. Small but devastatingly radioactive.
weaponPipeWrenchDescription: A heavy, metal, adjustable wrench, long enough to be used as a makeshift melee weapon. The adjustable jaws are often used to hold attachments, allowing the wrench to be modded extremely easily.
weaponPlasmaCasterDescription: An older, bulkier plasma weapon rediscovered after the Great War. It is more damaging and has a superior fire rate to a typical plasma rifle, but is far less accurate and much heavier. Note: Inflicts both Physical and Energy damage (use the lower resistance).
weaponPlasmaGrenadeDescription: A grenade filled with chemicals that rapidly transform into superheated plasma on detonation.
weaponPlasmaMineDescription: A magnetically-sealed mine that releases an energized wave of plasma when triggered.
weaponPlasmaPistolDescription: Plasma guns, or plasma casters, are high-tech weapons firing superheated ionized gas, or plasma. These weapons are rare and potent, delivering damage through the high-speed impact of the plasma bolt and then thermal transfer as the heat from the plasma bolt is transferred into the target at the moment of impact.
weaponPlasticExplosivesDescription: A very stable and malleable compound. Plastic explosives can be rigged to explode with either remote or timer detonators and can be detonated with other high explosives. Very useful for removing inconvenient nature.
weaponPoolCueDescription: A long, slender piece of wood used in games like pool. Useful as a melee weapon if there's nothing to hand with more heft.
weaponPowderChargeDescription: The powder charge is a crude improvised explosive consisting of a sealed tin can packed with dynamite, with a sensor module duct-taped to the side.
weaponPowerFistDescription: A Power Fist is a reinforced gauntlet or vambrace which mounts a heavy-duty pneumatic ram over the knuckles. Designed for use by demolition crews, it has also seen military use for breaching fortifications and clearing enemy barricades… and enemies.
weaponProtonAxeDescription: The Proton Axe resembles a futuristic war-axe with a glowing blue blade of electrical energy at its head. The intense electrical discharge it produces is especially damaging to robots and other mechanical foes.
weaponPulseGrenadeDescription: An electromagnetic pulse grenade that deals damage to robots, synths, and power armor.
weaponPulseMineDescription: A proximity-fused EMP mine that deals heavy damage to robots, synths, and power armor.
weaponPumpActionShotgunDescription: A solid and widespread design of shotgun found across the Wastelands. While it has a far larger magazine capacity than double-barreled shotguns, the slow (but distinctive) pump-action design keeps the rate of fire low compared to semi-automatic combat shotguns.
weaponRadiumRifleDescription: The Radium Rifle irradiates bullets as they are fired, causing ballistic damage, with an extra kick of radiation damage that is deadly to humans.
weaponRipperDescription: The Ripper is a small, handheld, militarized chainsaw that saw extensive use in the armed conflicts before the Great War, with a commercial-grade, scaled-down version made available for the general market. It can cut through flesh and metal with ease.
weaponRollingPinDescription: A sturdy cylinder of wood found in kitchens everywhere. Heavy enough to deter an assortment of attackers if you haven't got something more dangerous.
weaponShishkebabDescription: A makeshift 'flaming sword', the Shishkebab is found in various forms across the wastelands, with some built from an actual sword, while others are assembled from various components. All have some elements in common: some sort of tank or canister, a pilot light, and an ignition mechanism usually stolen from a motorcycle. When used, the fuel is released along the blade and ignited by the pilot light, wreathing the blade in flames.
weaponShredderDescription: A shredding barrel attachment on a minigun, used as a melee weapon in close combat.
weaponSledgehammerDescription: Sledgehammers are primitive weapons, used as tools before the Great War, for breaking ground or demolishing things. Although not usually considered as a powerful weapon, a strong person—or a super mutant—can wield one of these like a club, taking full advantage of the hammer's weight.
weaponSmokeGrenadeDescription: Smoke grenades produce a dense cloud of chemical smoke to screen for concealment. It adds +2 difficulty to all attacks and PER-based tests made by or against anyone within the cloud.
weaponSniperRifleDescription: A scoped rifle once designed for use by military and police forces to eliminate targets efficiently from long distances. The design incorporates many features that other weapons would be modded to have, but such built-in design means that it’s difficult to alter purpose-made sniper rifles.
weaponStoneDescription: A strike with a rock, a brick, etc.
weaponSuperSledgeDescription: Super Sledgehammers are powered variants of the regular sledgehammer. Older, pre-War designs were outfitted with kinetic storage devices to increase the impact force, while post-War ones mount a small rocket motor within the hammer's head to accelerate the swing and deliver a heavier blow.
weaponSwitchbladeDescription: The switchblade is a commonly found melee weapon. Its small size means that it is easily smuggled into restricted areas and is easy to handle. It doesn't do as damage as larger knives or blades, however.
weaponSwordDescription: Swords have been in use for almost as long as wars have been fought and remained widespread as symbols of status and prestige amongst soldiers—particularly officers—across the world right up until the Great War. Many of the swords found in the wasteland belonged to Chinese officers during the Great War, often taken from battlefields as a trophy by American troops. Others are museum pieces or replicas from private collections, with many resembling the cavalry sabers used during the American Revolution.
weaponSyringerDescription: A makeshift compressed-air weapon which fires customized syringes at a target. The syringes themselves do little damage, but can inflict a wide range of debilitating effects.
weaponTenMillimeterPistolDescription: Small, dependable, reasonably powerful, and widely available, the 10mm pistol has been a staple of wasteland combat since the bombs first fell. Its versatility and potential for customization mean that a well-looked-after 10mm pistol will keep its owner safe for a long time.
weaponTeslaCannonDescription: A shoulder-mounted directed energy weapon based on designs by Nikola Tesla. It is a rare sight in the Wastelands, though some branches of the Brotherhood of Steel have attempted to recreate the technology.
weaponTeslaRifleDescription: An odd blend of advanced technology and jury-rigged components, a Tesla Rifle can shoot deadly arcs of electricity that jump from enemy to enemy, injuring anyone caught nearby.
weaponThrowingKnivesDescription: Small, sharp blades designed to be thrown. Light, silent, and quick to use.
weaponTireIronDescription: A simple L-shaped tool found commonly in cars and garages; tire irons make for handy weapons when not being used to change the tires on a car.
weaponTomahawkDescription: A short-handled throwing axe. More imposing than a throwing knife and equally deadly.
weaponWalkingStickDescription: A simple stick used to support those who can't walk so well, a walking cane is sturdy enough to be an impromptu weapon if you've not got any other options.
weaponWarDrumDescription: Consisting of a metal drum mounted on a metal pole with spiked tin cans attached to the rim. Note: When used to make a sneak attack, it increases the complication range by 1 due to the sound it makes.
weaponWeaponStockDescription: A strike with the stock of a two-handed weapon.
weaponWeaponStockOneHandedDescription: A strike with the stock of a one-handed weapon.
